items.title: NPP declares Wontumi a ‘political prisoner’, vows to appeal conviction
items.description: The opposition New Patriotic Party (NPP) has described its Ashanti Regional Chairman, Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, as a ‘political prisoner’. This comes on the back of his conviction and 20-year prison sentence in an illegal mining case. Addressing a press conference on Monday, the party’s General Secretary, Justin Frimpong Kodua, said the NPP strongly disagrees with the High Court’s judgment and has already begun the process of challenging the decision at the Court of Appeal. “While we respect the authority of our courts, respect for the judiciary does not require silence in the face of a judgment that is fundamentally flawed,” Mr Kodua said, insisting that the Constitution guarantees every citizen the right to challenge judicial decisions through the appellate process. Chairman Wontumi is currently serving a 20-year prison sentence after the High Court found him guilty in connection with illegal mining activities involving Akonta Mining Limited. The prosecution argued that he unlawfully permitted third parties to mine on the company’s concession without the legally required ministerial approval. The NPP, however, maintains that the conviction was not supported by the evidence presented during the trial. According to Mr Kodua, it was never disputed that Wontumi lawfully acquired his mineral concession. He argued that the prosecution failed to prove that Wontumi had assigned or transferred his mineral rights, contending that the conviction was based on “assumptions and inferences” rather than proof beyond reasonable doubt. “We firmly believe that this conviction is a travesty of justice and has to be overturned on appeal,” he stated. The NPP also accused the governing National Democratic Congress (NDC) of selectively targeting political opponents while allegedly failing to act against individuals linked to illegal mining within its own ranks. Mr Kodua claimed the government was using the conviction as a “trophy” to demonstrate commitment to the fight against illegal mining while ignoring allegations involving members of the ruling party. He questioned the status of investigations into several individuals the NPP alleged had been linked to galamsey. He argued that justice must be applied fairly and consistently. “The fight against illegal mining cannot become credible only when it targets political opponents… while overlooking allegations involving others, especially from the President’s own party,” he said. Mr Kodua assured supporters that the NPP would stand by its Ashanti Regional Chairman throughout the legal process. “Our legal team has already commenced the necessary steps to challenge this judgment through the appellate process. The NPP considers Wontumi a POLITICAL PRISONER,” he declared.

items.title: Evala 2026 : Le chien et le coq, les animaux sacrificiels dans la Kozah et à Agbandè-Yaka
items.description: Kara, 20 juil. (ATOP)-Les cérémonies d’initiation des jeunes dans le cadre des luttes traditionnelles Evala diffèrent de par l’animal sacrificiel, selon qu’on soit dans les cantons de la préfecture de la Kozah ou dans celui d’Agbandè-Yaka, le seul canton qui lutte dans le Doufelgou. Quel animal est-il exigé dans les lieux sacrés le jour de l’initiation ? Les luttes des Evala sont des rites initiatiques pratiqués en pays Kabyè et dans le canton d’Agbandè-Yaka, dans la région de la Kara. Ces rites, meublés principalement par la lutte et d’autres cérémonies, marquent le passage à l’âge adulte pour les jeunes de 18 à 20 ans. Cependant, les animaux sacrificiels diffèrent d’un canton à l’autre, même si le rôle de l’oncle reste inchangé. Le chien exigé chez les Kabyè D’après l’encadreur de lutte, Tagba Essouhouna, dans la tradition kabyè, le chien est un élément central de l’initiation (Evala). Il souligne que son rôle est primordial car la viande est consommée par les jeunes lutteurs pour leur transmettre le courage, la ruse, la rage de vaincre et l’endurance nécessaires pour affronter leurs adversaires et devenir des hommes. Le coq recommandé à Agbandè-Yaka Si dans la Kozah, le chien est obligatoirement requis pour les cérémonies d’initiation du jeune Evalou (singulier de Evala) qui dure plusieurs jours, dans le canton d’Agbandè-Yaka, c’est plutôt le coq qui est exigé dans les lieux sacrés pour l’initiation qui ne dure qu’une seule journée. « Dans notre localité, comme nous ne mangeons pas le chien, c’est le coq qui est recommandé dans les lieux de cérémonies où les jeunes lutteurs sont initiés par les Egoulou », explique le régent du canton d’Agbandè-Yaka, Simtagna Mabeou. Le rôle de l’oncle inchangé Les deux intervenants ont fait savoir que, généralement, c’est l’oncle maternel qui a la lourde responsabilité de se procurer l’animal sacrificiel pour son neveu. Toutefois, disent-ils, le père peut intervenir pour celui-ci s’il n’a pas les moyens. A les en croire, si les familles ne peuvent pas se procurer le chien ou le coq pour les initiés, les cérémonies des garçons sont reportées à l’année suivante. L’organisation des luttes Une fois ces initiations terminées en pays Kabyè comme dans le canton d’Agbandè-Yaka, les lutteurs sont soumis, le reste des jours, aux entrainements. Ces essais permettent de sélectionner et de classer dans les rangs, les meilleurs qui s’affrontent d’abord en préliminaires par quartiers et villages avant que les lutteurs ne se regroupent pour se mesurer à d’autres coalitions en finale. ATOP/MEK/KYA

items.title: Las celebraciones de los campeones del mundo, en directo | Felipe VI felicita a los jugadores en el Palacio de la Zarzuela: “Ha sido un triunfo épico”
items.description: Los reyes Felipe VI y Letizia, la princesa Leonor y la infanta Sofía han recibido a los jugadores de la selección española en el Palacio de la Zarzuela para felicitarlos por su triunfo este domingo en la final del Mundial . “Ha sido un triunfo épico”, ha afirmado el Rey. Los jugadores han aterrizado este lunes en torno a las 14.30 en el aeropuerto Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas, donde entre 200 y 300 personas los esperaban con camisetas de España, banderas al cuello y teléfonos móviles. Tras la recepción en Zarzuela, los futbolistas se dirigen a La Moncloa. Después, iniciarán el recorrido en autobús descubierto con el que celebrará su victoria con los aficionados. La celebración será en Cibeles y su entorno, y se espera que se acerquen más de 60.000 personas a celebrar la victoria con la selección española. Por otro lado, la FIFA ha anunciado que abrirá una investigación a la selección argentina por los incidentes protagonizados por varios de sus jugadores y un miembro del cuerpo técnico cuando los jugadores de la Roja celebraban en el campo su victoria. items.title: Lagos pays N3.1bn compensation to landowners affected by public projects
items.description: By Olasunkanmi Akoni LAGOS — The Lagos State Government has disbursed over ₦3.1 billion as compensation to more than 300 landowners whose properties were acquired for various public infrastructure projects across the state. Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu, who presented the compensation cheques to the beneficiaries on Monday at Alausa, Ikeja, said the exercise reflected the administration’s commitment to responsible governance, fairness and respect for the rights of residents. The governor, who was accompanied by his deputy, Dr. Obafemi Hamzat, described the payment as the fulfilment of the government’s legal and moral responsibility to citizens whose lands were acquired for projects serving the public interest. Members of the State Executive Council, traditional rulers and community leaders attended the event. Sanwo-Olu stressed that the compensation was not a gesture of charity but an obligation rooted in justice, adding that infrastructure development must always be balanced with transparency and protection of citizens’ interests. He explained that all claims were subjected to due process, including enumeration, professional valuation and verification of ownership documents, to ensure that only legitimate beneficiaries received payment. According to him, the acquired properties were earmarked for strategic infrastructure projects designed to improve the living standards of Lagos residents, assuring that the lands would not be diverted for personal interests. The governor advised beneficiaries to use the funds wisely by investing in sustainable ventures that would provide long-term benefits for themselves and their families. He also assured residents that officials of the Lands Bureau remained available to provide guidance on documentation and other related matters without demanding any form of gratification. Earlier, Deputy Governor Hamzat said the compensation payment demonstrated the administration’s determination to honour its commitments to residents affected by government projects. He noted that while development initiatives sometimes require the acquisition of private properties, the government would continue to ensure that affected owners receive appropriate compensation in line with the law. The Permanent Secretary of the Lagos State Lands Bureau, Kamar Olowoshago, said the exercise was part of ongoing compensation payments by the government, adding that it underscored the administration’s commitment to transparency, accountability and inclusive development. Olowoshago noted that meaningful development must not come at the expense of justice and the welfare of citizens. The Chairman of the Lagos State House of Assembly Committee on Lands, Hon. Olayinka Kazeem, commended the initiative, describing it as a reflection of a government that prioritises the welfare of its people. The compensation exercise is part of efforts by the Lagos State Government to ensure that infrastructure expansion is achieved while safeguarding the rights and interests of residents affected by public projects. The post Lagos pays N3.1bn compensation to landowners affected by public projects appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods
items.description: Chinese online retail giant AliExpress has been fined a record €550m (£467m) by the EU for allowing the sale of illegal products such as unsafe toys and fake clothes. The European Commission said AliExpress has fallen short of its legal obligations “diligently assess” the risk of illegal, unsafe or fake goods on its platform. “The spread of counterfeit clothing, unsafe toys, dangerous cosmetics and other illegal and harmful products is not an unavoidable cost of shopping online – it is a failure by AliExpress to comply with its obligations,” said EU tech chief Henna Virkkunen. AliExpress said the fine was “disproportionate” and that it would appeal. The two-year investigation found that AliExpress’s detection systems “did not work properly”, with many illegal products not flagged while others that were identified remained on the site for several weeks. The European Commission also found the company did not properly enforce penalties on traders selling illegal goods. Furthermore, its product compliance checks could be “easily circumvented”. AliExpress, which is owned by the Chinese tech conglomerate Alibaba, has 193 million users in Europe, more than fellow Chinese online retailers Shein or Temu. The penalty is the highest fine imposed under the Digital Services Act, which requires tech giants to do more to counter illegal and harmful content. The act allows for fines of up to 6% of a company’s revenue, but as Alibaba had a global turnover of €122bn last year, the penalty is far short of that. AliExpress said it was surprised by and disagreed with the EU’s decision. “AliExpress has been and continues to be committed to meeting our obligations to consumers. We invest substantial resources in risk assessment and mitigation, product safety and consumer protection,” it said. “Today’s decision and disproportionate fine ignores our sound risk management framework and the significant, proactive enhancements we have made. We will appeal the decision.” Under the EU’s ruling, the company has to pay the penalty and present a plan by 20 October that includes what action it will take to tackle the breaches. Earlier this year, Temu was given a €200m fine for allowing the sale of illegal products such as dangerous baby toys. And Elon Musk’s X was fined €120m last year, after the commission said allowing users to pay for its blue tick badges was deceptive because the social media platform was not “meaningfully verifying” who was behind the account and exposed users to scams.

items.title: Nouvel An kamit : Le cadre 2HK réitère sa demande d’officialisation du calendrier africain
items.description: Après cinq jours de conférences et de ciné-débats, les Kamites du Burkina Faso ont clôturé, le dimanche 19 juillet 2026 à Ouagadougou, la XIe édition des Universités SANKORÉ, marquée par la célébration du Nouvel An kamit (1er Djehouty 6263). À cette occasion, Lianhoué Imhotep Bayala et ses camarades ont renouvelé leur appel à la reconnaissance officielle du calendrier africain. Depuis 2015, chaque 19 juillet, correspondant au 1er Djehouty du calendrier africain, marque le début d'une nouvelle année pour la communauté kamite. Le cadre Deux Heures pour Nous, Deux Heures pour Kamita (2HK) a célébré pour la onzième fois le Nouvel An africain, qui en est à sa 6263e année. Selon son coordinateur national, Lianhoué Imhotep Bayala, cette célébration dépasse le simple cadre d'une fête. Elle constitue, d'après lui, un acte de mémoire, de souveraineté culturelle et de réaffirmation de l'identité de l'Homme noir. Lianhoué Imhotep Bayala demandant aux plus hautes autorités la reconnaissance du calendrier africain À l'en croire, chaque peuple digne de ce nom s'appuie sur ses propres repères historiques pour construire son avenir. Lianhoué Imhotep Bayala regrette que les références temporelles africaines aient été marginalisées au profit de calendriers hérités d'autres civilisations. Pour M. Bayala, il ne s'agit pas de rejeter les autres cultures, mais de redonner à l'Afrique le droit de raconter son histoire avec ses propres références. Cet objectif est notamment poursuivi à travers les Universités SANKORÉ, conformément aux enseignements de grands penseurs africains tels que Cheikh Anta Diop, Théophile Obenga et d'autres chercheurs qui ont démontré l'ancienneté et la richesse des civilisations africaines. « Les Universités SANKORÉ ont enseigné que la renaissance africaine ne saurait être uniquement économique. Elle doit aussi être intellectuelle et symbolique. Restaurer un calendrier africain, qui existait 4 236 ans avant Jésus-Christ, participe de cette dynamique de reconstruction des imaginaires collectifs. Un peuple qui maîtrise son temps, son histoire et ses références renforce sa confiance en lui-même et prépare les générations futures à assumer leurs responsabilités dans le concert des nations », a-t-il déclaré. À ceux qui pensent que la promotion du Nouvel An africain ne serait qu'une aspiration idéaliste ou une simple vue de l'esprit, Lianhoué Imhotep Bayala a indiqué que les faits apportent aujourd'hui un démenti « éloquent ». Le coordinateur de 2HK a lancé un appel à tous les Africains pour qu'ils investissent les langues africaines afin de trouver toutes les déclinaisons de ce calendrier dans chaque rameau culturel. Il a également appelé le Chef de l'État à reconnaître officiellement ce calendrier. Pour sa part, le président du comité d'organisation, Beyirnè Fidèle Dabiré, a salué une organisation qui a tenu toutes ses promesses. Selon lui, les activités inscrites au programme ont connu un franc succès. « Le 19 juillet, bien qu'étant la date de la célébration du Nouvel An kamit, constitue l'aboutissement des activités susmentionnées. Si ces activités, dans leur majorité, ont connu un succès éclatant, cela est à mettre à l'actif des différents comités qui n'ont ménagé aucun effort pour la réussite de ces Universités SANKORÉ », a fait savoir Beyirnè Fidèle Dabiré, exprimant sa gratitude aux parrains et aux organisations partenaires. Un aperçu du public Représentant le parrain, le Professeur Moumouni Zoungrana, le docteur Barthélémy Kaboré a salué la pertinence de la thématique générale. De son avis, le thème retenu cette année, « Industrialisation et opportunités démographiques au Sahel à l'ère des fractures géopolitiques », est d'une grande actualité, car les bouleversements géopolitiques que connaît le monde imposent aux pays africains de repenser leur modèle de développement. « À cet égard, les actions menées par Deux Heures pour Kamita s'inscrivent en parfaite complémentarité avec les ambitions nationales en matière d'éducation. Elles rejoignent également l'esprit de l'Initiative présidentielle pour une éducation de qualité, qui vise à bâtir un système éducatif fondé sur l'excellence, le civisme, le patriotisme, la responsabilité et la valorisation de nos références culturelles », a-t-il apprécié. Notons que les principales innovations du calendrier 6263 ont été l'introduction des noms des mois en mooré ainsi que des 42 lois de la Maât. Lire aussi : Universités Sankoré 2026 : la 11e édition s’ouvre autour de l’industrialisation au Sahel Jean-François SOMÉ Minute.bf

items.title: Ten killed as Russian attacks on merchant ships in Black Sea intensify
items.description: The death toll of a Russian strike on a cargo ship off the coast of the Ukrainian city of Odesa has risen to 10. The Guinea-Bissau-flagged ship was hit by Russian cruise missiles on Sunday, the Ukrainian Air Force said, causing a fire. A search and rescue operation was launched and lasted all night. By Monday morning, Ukraine’s seaports authority said nine crew members had been killed, as well as one of its pilots. In recent weeks, the Odesa region has come under sustained attack. Twenty-eight people died in Russian strikes on the region in July alone, regional head Oleh Kiper told Ukrainian TV on Monday. Russian attacks on cargo ships in the Black Sea have also increased. Sunday’s attack on the Guinea-Bissau-flagged Golden Leo ship was the deadliest of its kind. These usually foreign-flagged ships carrying international crews sustain Ukraine’s last remaining maritime export corridor to transport cargo down the Romanian and Bulgarian coastlines and onwards to Turkey. Maritime exports account for over two-thirds of Ukrainian agricultural exports, for a value of roughly $9bn (£6.7bn) – an essential source of revenue for Kyiv. Fighting in the Black Sea was at a stalemate for most of the four-and-a-half years since Moscow first launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. While Ukrainian ships were unable to move further than the area surrounding Odesa due to Russian control of much of the coast, Kyiv’s drones forced Moscow to keep its assets safely tucked in its naval bases in the eastern Black Sea. But Russia’s ramping up of attacks against cargo ships is likely meant to deter foreign companies from buying Ukrainian products and using its Black Sea maritime corridor. On Sunday, Ukraine’s Prime Minister Serhiy Koretskyi said his government and the military command were “preparing decisions that will enhance the safety of navigation and help protect civilian vessels and seafarers” from Russian attacks. Despite the constant shelling, Odesa remains a prime holiday destination for Ukrainians. Governor Oleh Kiper said earlier this month that air raid shelters had been set up in 40 beaches in the region which were open to the public – but warned that seaside locations could be particularly dangerous because drones and missiles are often launched from the sea. For its part, Ukraine too has ramped up its attacks on Russian assets in the Black Sea and the Sea of Azov. On Monday, Ukraine’s drone forces commander Robert Brovdi said 183 vessels had been struck in July. While this figure has not been independently verified, the BBC has seen evidence of damage to numerous vessels in both the Black Sea and the Sea of Azov on satellite. Elsewhere in the country, Russian strikes killed five people in separate attacks on eastern and southern Ukrainian cities. According to the UN, June saw the highest number of Ukrainian civilians killed and injured since April 2022. At least 293 civilians were killed and 1,990 injured. For its part, Kyiv also continued to hit logistics facilities and an oil depot in Russia’s Moscow region. Sergei Sobyanin, the mayor of the Russian capital, said Kyiv had launched 400 drones towards the region overnight on Monday, although most were shot down. Local authorities later said 10 people had been injured as a result. And local authorities in Russia’s Belgorod border region said a Ukrainian drone strike on a bus had killed five people, including a child, on Monday. Ukraine has not yet commented.

items.title: Côte d’Ivoire : six responsables locaux du principal parti d’opposition libérés (avocat)
items.description: Six responsables locaux de la principale force d'opposition ivoirienne, le Parti démocratique de Côte d'Ivoire (PDCI), ont été libérés lundi après neuf mois de détention, a annoncé leur avocat. Des dizaines de membres de l'opposition ont été arrêtés en 2025 en Côte d'Ivoire, année d'une élection présidentielle au climat politique tendu. « À l'issue de l'audience de la chambre antiterroriste de ce lundi 20 juillet, mes clients et militants du PDCI (Allo Pacôme Didier, Kouleon Manhenin, N'Cho Gnangoran, Mabo Koffi Jean-Marc, Ouattara Kinjinlman, Niangoran N'Guessan Édouard) recouvrent la liberté après 9 mois de détention et rentrent enfin chez eux », a écrit sur Facebook leur avocat, Me Ange Rodrigue Dadje. Contacté par l'AFP, il n'a pas souhaité préciser les raisons de leur libération. En mai, deux autres militants du PDCI, Clément Kouakou et Denis Brou, avaient également été libérés. Ces huit membres du PDCI, des responsables locaux du parti dans plusieurs communes d'Abidjan, avaient été arrêtés entre fin septembre et début octobre 2025, mois d'une élection présidentielle très critiquée. Des figures de l'opposition, comme le président du PDCI, Tidjane Thiam, ou l'ex-chef d'État Laurent Gbagbo, ont été exclues du scrutin à la suite de décisions de justice. Plusieurs dizaines de militants de différents partis d'opposition, parfois des cadres, ont été arrêtés ou écroués. En outre, des centaines de personnes accusées d'avoir participé à des manifestations interdites lors de la campagne ont été condamnées et une dizaine de personnes sont mortes. Les autorités avaient assuré que les interpellations n'avaient aucun lien avec l'appartenance politique et respectaient des procédures d'enquête régulières. Début juillet, un ex-ministre de la Défense de Laurent Gbagbo et membre de son parti, Moïse Lida Kouassi, a été libéré après une détention de près d'un an. Un cybermilitant proche du même camp politique, Ibrahim Zigui, a également été remis en liberté. AFP

items.title: 2027: Muslim-Muslim ticket not anti-Christian, Tinubu has demonstrated religious tolerance — Hazzan
items.description: By James Ogunnaike, Abeokuta A former Deputy Speaker of the Ogun State House of Assembly and Assistant Pastor of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG), Pastor Remmy Hazzan, has defended the All Progressives Congress (APC)’s decision to retain a Muslim-Muslim presidential ticket ahead of the 2027 general elections, arguing that the arrangement does not undermine Nigeria’s religious diversity. Hazzan, who is also a member of the APC, made the remarks during an interview on Frontline, a current affairs programme aired by Eagle 102.5 FM, Ilese-Ijebu, Ogun State. He dismissed concerns that the party’s decision could marginalise Christians, saying President Bola Tinubu had demonstrated religious tolerance through his personal relationships and leadership style. “We are not religious bigots. We do not promote anything that favours one religion over another or threatens the existence of any faith,” Hazzan said. The cleric pointed to President Tinubu’s marriage to Senator Oluremi Tinubu, an ordained pastor of the RCCG, as an indication of his respect for religious harmony. “If such a man has lived peacefully with his Christian wife for decades, that shows his level of religious tolerance, and we believe that disposition will continue to reflect in his leadership of Nigeria,” he said. Hazzan maintained that the APC’s decision to adopt a Muslim-Muslim ticket was based on political considerations rather than religious exclusion. According to him, the emergence of a Southern Muslim as the presidential candidate of a major political party created a unique political situation that required a different strategy. “This is the first time a Southern Muslim is emerging as the candidate of a major political party. In previous elections involving Southern Christian candidates, it was easier to select Muslim running mates from the North. This time, choosing a Northern Christian would have required extensive political negotiations within the party and among voters,” he said. He argued that the decision should not be viewed as an indication of disregard for Christians, stressing that political realities influenced the choice. “It took courage for President Bola Tinubu to make that decision. It was never because there was any disregard for the Christian faith. It was simply what the party considered the most practical political option under the circumstances,” he stated. The former lawmaker also rejected claims that the APC considered Christian voters politically insignificant, saying the issue was about electoral strategy and not religious exclusion. Hazzan advocated that leadership selection should be based on competence, character and integrity rather than religious identity. “The best of us should rule the rest of us, not the worst ruling the best. Christians should present their best, Muslims should present their best, and even traditional religion adherents should present their best. When the best emerge from every group, society benefits,” he said. Tinubu’s Reforms Require Time — Hazzan Assessing the Tinubu administration after three years in office, Hazzan acknowledged that some government policies had brought temporary hardship to Nigerians but expressed optimism that the country was beginning to witness economic recovery. He urged Nigerians to give the administration more time to complete ongoing reforms, saying the difficult measures were necessary to reposition the economy. “The current administration has done so much. We are beginning to approach equilibrium, and it is becoming clearer that those difficult periods were necessary if Nigeria is to return to prosperity. Mr. President should be allowed to complete the work he has started,” he said. On the involvement of Christians in politics, Hazzan urged believers to take active interest in governance, rejecting the notion that politics should be avoided by religious people. “For a long time, the church was made to believe politics is dirty and only dirty people should participate. But when something is dirty, it needs cleaning. You clean it better from within than from outside,” he said. He added that Christians in public office must remain guided by their moral values and principles. “Politics does not have to corrupt believers. Public office holders must never forget the values they embraced before assuming office,” Hazzan said. The post 2027: Muslim-Muslim ticket not anti-Christian, Tinubu has demonstrated religious tolerance — Hazzan appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Motor rider killed in accident at Abenaso
items.description: A young man identified as Sammy, popularly known as Tompoo, has lost his life in a tragic motorbike accident at Abenaso in the Birim North District of the Eastern Region. The incident occurred when Sammy was reportedly riding through the area and encountered a slippery pothole on the road. According to reports, the impact caused the motorbike to skid, resulting in him losing control and crashing. The severity of the accident left him with critical injuries. Bystanders and residents who rushed to the scene quickly arranged for him to be transported to the New Abirem Government Hospital for medical attention, where he was confirmed dead. Some residents have also expressed concerns about the condition of roads in the area, particularly the presence of potholes, which they say pose serious risks to motorists and other road users. They are appealing to authorities responsible for road maintenance to take urgent steps to fix damaged sections of roads to help prevent similar accidents. Meanwhile, further details surrounding the accident, including the exact circumstances leading to the crash, remain unclear.

items.title: Tears for the Zenith: The Final curtain falls on Takoradi’s musical shrine
items.description: When I heard somewhere last week that the famous Zenith Hotel was being demolished, I wept for my country. I am not ashamed to admit it. I sat in my living room, phone in hand, staring at the news alert, and the tears just came. It felt like losing a relative—someone who had been part of my story even if I never met them in person. I have always maintained that we could have maintained the building well to act as a museum or something of that sort. But we didn’t. We let it rot. We let it become a shadow of its former self. And now, the wrecking ball is coming. The music is finally dying. Last week, the Sekondi-Takoradi Metropolitan Directorate of NADMO confirmed what many of us had dreaded: the Zenith Hotel building in Takoradi has been selected as the first structure to be pulled down under a demolition exercise targeting 88 dilapidated buildings across the metropolis I understand the safety concerns. I really do. But this is not just a building. This is sacred ground. This is where our music was born. The Home of Legends Let me tell you what the Zenith Hotel meant. In the late 1950s, when Ghana was still finding its feet as an independent nation, a man named J.K. Agyepong—who also ran the Prempeh Cinema—had a vision. In 1958, he did something extraordinary: he fully financed and housed a resident big band, the Broadway Dance Band, founded by Nigerian trumpeter Sam Obote. It became the first big band of its kind in Africa. Think about that for a moment. In a small coastal city like Takoradi, a hotel owner decided to invest in music. Not because it was profitable—but because he believed in the power of sound. The Broadway Dance Band played ballroom jazz, blues, and highlife. By the early 1960s, they were considered a national orchestra, touring with President Kwame Nkrumah himself. When Ghana Airways was inaugurated, the band performed in Khartoum and Beirut. They put Ghana on the map, not with politics or gold, but with music. The name “Broadway” was later changed to Uhuru in 1964 after the hotel management sued the band over the use of the name. The musicians became the legendary Uhuru Dance Band—a 24-piece orchestra that would go on to become one of Ghana’s most famous musical exports. The musicians who played there were national treasures. And the hotel was their home. Where Talent Was Born But the Zenith Hotel wasn’t just a performance venue. It was a nursery for talent. Highlife icon Pat Thomas learned his craft there, and it was there that he got his big break. The story goes like this: a young Pat Thomas walked into a rehearsal at the hotel, approached the band, and began to sing. He wasn’t invited. He wasn’t announced. He just walked in and opened his mouth. His delivery was so powerful that it stunned the entire room. The musicians stopped playing. They just stared. And in that moment, a legend was born. That is what the Zenith Hotel did. It created moments like that. It was a place where talent met opportunity, where a young boy with a dream could walk into a room and change his life forever. The rooms of the Zenith Hotel had seen so much. They had seen the backs of travelling musicians and seafarers and dignitaries. They had seen the backs of many women who plied their trade around the area. They had held laughter and tears, joy and sorrow. They were alive. A Demolition of History Now, NADMO has earmarked the Zenith Hotel and the nearby Prempeh Cinema building for demolition. Final technical assessments are currently underway, and officials say the exercise could begin within a matter of days. I understand the practicalities. I understand that buildings deteriorate. I understand that safety matters. But what I don’t understand is how we got here. How did we let a national treasure become a squatter’s shelter? How did we let the walls that once echoed with jazz trumpets and highlife guitars become a danger to the public? A Museum That Never Was For years, I have argued that the Zenith Hotel should have been preserved—not as a hotel, but as a museum. A monument to the golden age of Ghanaian highlife. Imagine it. Tourists from all over the world coming to Takoradi to see where Pat Thomas sang his first notes, where the Broadway Dance Band rehearsed, where President Nkrumah’s orchestra was born. Imagine schoolchildren visiting to learn about their musical heritage. Imagine the pride we would feel. Instead, we let it rot. We let it become a dilapidated shell. And now, safety concerns have given us no choice but to pull it down. We could have saved it. We should have saved it. But we didn’t. The Silence of the Bricks When the demolition begins, the walls of the Zenith Hotel will fall—walls that once echoed with jazz trumpets and highlife guitars. Walls that heard the laughter of musicians and the whispers of lovers. Walls that held the dreams of a young nation finding its voice. When I heard the news, I wept for my country. Not because we are demolishing a building, but because we are demolishing our memory. We are choosing practicality over history, convenience over legacy. And when the dust settles, all that will remain of the Zenith Hotel is what we carry in our hearts: the music, the legends, and the tears for what we let slip away. I will remember the Zenith Hotel. I will remember the joy it brought, the talent it nurtured, the history it held. And I will ask myself: what else are we letting slip away? What other treasures are we ignoring until it’s too late? The Zenith Hotel is gone. But the music—the music will never die. It lives in every highlife song we sing, every dance we dance, every memory we hold. But oh, what a loss. What a terrible, avoidable loss.

items.title: NBA: Electoral reform to top my agenda, says president-elect Badejo-Okusanya, SAN
items.description: … receives certificate of return By Ikechukwu Nnochiri ABUJA— The President-elect of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA), Mrs. Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, has pledged to embark on a holistic reform of the association’s electoral process. Speaking in Abuja on Monday, shortly after she was issued a Certificate of Return by the Chairman of the NBA Electoral Committee (ECNBA), Chief Aham Ejelam, SAN, Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, acknowledged complaints trailing her emergence as the 33rd President of the association. She stressed that her resolve to reform the legal body’s electoral process was driven not only by misgivings among her co-contestants, “but because there is a perception among the wider membership of the Bar that our elections are always rigged.” She said: “Unfortunately, that perception, even though it lacks validation, has assumed the toga of reality. So we all need to sit down together — call it a referendum, call it a discussion — among members. “We must have an honest conversation about how elections should go going forward, because any election that keeps some people raising the same dust every election cycle is not healthy for the growth of any association. “So I will certainly be making electoral reform a priority so that we will have a system that everybody respects and agrees with. This is not to deny the fact that we cannot have a foolproof system. “Not everybody will be happy, but we want one that the majority of members respect and accept,” she added. Mrs. Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, also used the opportunity to extend an olive branch to those who contested the NBA top job with her, saying she plans to visit them and ask them to join her in moving the association forward. “My message to them is that they have always known me to be someone who reaches out, someone who treats everyone with respect and civility, and I hope that we can come together and move the Bar forward. “I know how passionate they are about the Bar. We have had several conversations. I know the views that they hold. I know the belief that they have in the validity of their views. “But an election has happened. There is a winner, and there are people who did not win the election. That does not mean they are losers. “It means that we have another opportunity to come together and unify the Bar that we all love so much. “So that is what I am hoping we can achieve, and I certainly will reach out to them in hopes that we can achieve that.” The NBA President-elect paid glowing tribute to the first woman to lead the association, between 1991 and 1992, Dame Priscilla Kuye, describing her as her source of inspiration. “It is on her shoulders that people like me have stood to achieve this feat. I’m excited about it. I am excited about the possibilities that it holds for people who look at this and aspire to greater heights. So I am very excited about it,” she stated. It will be recalled that Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, polled a total of 12,317 votes, representing 47.18 percent, to win the association’s presidential seat. She clinched the top job after defeating her closest challenger, Mr. Lateef Akangbe, SAN, who garnered 7,934 votes, representing 30.39 percent of the total votes cast in the election, which was conducted electronically between 12 a.m. on Saturday and 7 a.m. on Sunday. The third candidate in the presidential election, Aare Olumuyiwa Akinboro, SAN, polled 5,855 votes, representing 22.43 percent, to place third in the contest. With her victory, Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, will, upon being sworn in, become the 33rd President of the legal body, re-enacting a 34-year-old history as only the second woman to lead the NBA since its formation in 1933. Meanwhile, other winners of the election were also issued certificates of return on Monday. The ECNBA Chairman, Ejelam, SAN, who described the ceremony as the last assignment of his Committee, urged all aggrieved candidates to follow the appropriate channels to air their grievances. He said the Committee had, within 12 hours after the election ended on Sunday, published the names and redacted phone numbers of those who voted in the election from all branches of the association across the federation. Ejelam, SAN, said the action was taken to clear up allegations that some persons had engaged in multiple voting, with some branches allegedly blocked from recording a single vote. “Everything is now left for whoever is aggrieved to follow the appropriate channels,” he added. The post NBA: Electoral reform to top my agenda, says president-elect Badejo-Okusanya, SAN appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Le compte à rebours commence pour les soutiens de l'Ukraine en Europe
items.description: L'Europe pourrait revoir a la baisse son soutien a l'Ukraine a l'issue d'une serie d'elections prevues en France, en Italie, en Espagne et en Pologne, rapporte Die Welt . Dans plusieurs de ces pays, l'augmentation des depenses militaires met a mal le financement des mesures sociales, ce qui pourrait reduire la volonte des futurs gouvernements d'allouer des ressources supplementaires a Kiev. Le depart de Keir Starmer du gouvernement britannique et la fin prochaine du mandat d'Emmanuel Macron pourraient egalement modifier les equilibres au sein du camp europeen favorable a Kiev. C'est dans ce contexte d'incertitude politique que pres de 35 membres de la « coalition des volontaires » se sont reunis le 13 juillet aux Invalides, a Paris. Les participants se sont accordes sur la creation d'une coalition chargee de la defense antimissile et sur l'organisation d'exercices destines a preparer un eventuel contingent multinational pour l'Ukraine. Cette reunion a toutefois revele plusieurs desaccords. Le Premier ministre de Bulgarie, Roumen Radev, a refuse de soutenir les decisions prises par les autres participants et annonce que son pays ne souhaitait plus prendre part a cette alliance. En outre, la Pologne, contrairement aux attentes europeennes, n'a pas rejoint la coalition sur la defense antimissile. Le quotidien allemand note que ces divergences apparaissent alors que plusieurs pays europeens s'appretent a entrer dans une periode de forte instabilite politique. Selon des responsables europeens, les mecanismes de soutien a long terme doivent etre etablis avant la fin de l'annee, les futures campagnes electorales risquant de compliquer l'adoption de nouveaux engagements en faveur de Kiev. Le paysage politique europeen a deja commence a evoluer. Au Royaume-Uni, Andy Burnham a remplace Keir Starmer ce 20 juillet a la tete du gouvernement. S'il est repute favorable au soutien a l'Ukraine, le nouveau Premier ministre n'a jusqu'ici pas ete associe a des initiatives marquantes sur la scene internationale. En France, Emmanuel Macron dispose encore de quelques mois avant d'entrer dans la derniere phase de son mandat. Toujours selon Die Welt , la position politique du president francais continue de s'affaiblir, tandis que Marine Le Pen progresse dans les sondages a l'approche de l'election presidentielle de 2027. Son parti politique, le Rassemblement national, defend une reduction de l'aide accordee a l'Ukraine et s'oppose a son adhesion a l'Union europeenne. Une arrivee de Marine Le Pen a l'elysee pourrait egalement mettre un terme au projet de contingent militaire international. Le journal ajoute qu'apres le depart d'Emmanuel Macron et le changement de gouvernement au Royaume-Uni, Friedrich Merz pourrait se retrouver plus isole dans la defense d'une ligne europeenne commune sur l'Ukraine. Rien ne garantit toutefois que le chancelier allemand parvienne a federer les partenaires de Berlin et a preserver leur niveau actuel de soutien a Kiev.

items.title: Ondo Labour Unions threaten indefinite strike at FUTATH over IPPIS, workers’ rights dispute
items.description: By Dayo Johnson, Akure The Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC) and the Trade Union Congress (TUC) in Ondo State have issued a seven-day ultimatum to the management of the Federal University of Technology Akure Teaching Hospital (FUTATH), threatening an indefinite strike over alleged violations of workers’ rights and breaches of the transition agreement. The warning was contained in a joint letter signed by the Ondo State NLC Chairman, Comrade Prince Ademola Olapade, and TUC Chairman, Comrade Clement Fatuase, and addressed to the Chief Medical Director (CMD) of FUTATH, Prof. Olusegun Ojo. The unions accused the hospital management of failing to fully implement the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) guiding the transition of the former State Specialist Hospital into FUTATH, warning that the alleged infractions could disrupt industrial harmony at the institution. According to the labour unions, the ultimatum followed resolutions reached during a strategic meeting involving organised labour and health sector unions in FUTATH on July 16. They alleged that workers listed in the authenticated nominal roll attached to the transition MoU had not all been enrolled on the Integrated Payroll and Personnel Information System (IPPIS), despite previous agreements. The unions also accused the management of compelling some inherited workers to sign documents renouncing their membership of recognised trade unions as a condition for participation in the transition process. They described the alleged action as a violation of workers’ constitutional right to freedom of association and international labour standards. The labour bodies further faulted an alleged claim by the CMD that the nominal roll attached to the MoU had been “loaded,” insisting that the issue had already been addressed during a stakeholders’ meeting involving Ondo State Governor Lucky Aiyedatiwa, the Vice-Chancellor of the Federal University of Technology, Akure (FUTA), organised labour and other stakeholders. “Salary records presented during the meeting validated the staff strength contained in the nominal roll, making any contrary assertion reckless, unfortunate and capable of eroding confidence in the transition process,” the unions stated. They also accused the management of intimidation and victimisation of labour leaders and members over legitimate union activities. The unions demanded the immediate enrolment of all workers captured in the authenticated nominal roll on IPPIS, an end to alleged forced renunciation of union membership, cessation of intimidation of labour representatives and full implementation of the transition MoU. They warned that failure to address the issues within seven days would compel them to activate lawful industrial action measures, including an indefinite strike that could affect healthcare services at the teaching hospital. The unions also said they would notify relevant authorities that the continued stay of the CMD in office had become an obstacle to industrial peace if the alleged violations persisted. Copies of the letter were sent to the Presidency, Minister of Health, Governor Aiyedatiwa, Ondo State Commissioner for Health, the Attorney-General of the Federation, and security agencies including the police, Department of State Services (DSS) and Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC). CMD: Transition Process Follows Due Process Reacting to the allegations, the Chief Medical Director of FUTATH, Prof. Olusegun Ojo, said the transition process was being carried out in accordance with the provisions of the MoU. Ojo explained that only workers approved by the Federal Ministry of Health after verification would be absorbed into the federal establishment. He disclosed that more than 800 workers had already been captured on IPPIS, adding that a mop-up exercise had also been conducted for those who missed the initial verification process. According to him, the disagreement arose from a misunderstanding of the transition agreement, which he said did not provide for the automatic absorption of all workers. “Over 800 workers have been captured under IPPIS, while a mop-up exercise has been conducted for those who missed the initial exercise,” he said. The CMD appealed to labour leaders to use established channels for resolving grievances, assuring that the hospital management remained committed to a transparent transition process and ensuring that all approved workers were enrolled on the federal payroll as quickly as possible. The post Ondo Labour Unions threaten indefinite strike at FUTATH over IPPIS, workers’ rights dispute appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Wontumi’s conviction legitimate, more galamsey prosecutions must follow – Prof Agyemang-Duah
items.description: Governance expert, Prof Baffour Agyemang-Duah, says the conviction of New Patriotic Party (NPP) Ashanti Regional Chairman Bernard Antwi-Boasiako is a legitimate outcome of the judicial process but must be followed by further prosecutions of other individuals linked to illegal mining. His comments follow the Accra High Court’s conviction and sentencing of NPP officer, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, to 20 years’ imprisonment over illegal mining activities on the Samreboi concession in the Western Region. Speaking on JoyNews’ The Pulse , Prof. Agyemang-Duah said the issue of illegal mining, popularly known as galamsey, has become a national concern due to its devastating impact on Ghana’s environment and the future of the country. He noted that for several years, Ghanaians have raised concerns about the activities of illegal miners across the country, but there have been few convictions involving individuals widely known to be behind such operations. According to him, the court’s decision in the Wontumi case demonstrates that the legal process can hold people accountable when the appropriate procedures are followed. “I believe strongly that this case went through the normal procedures and processes of the court, and therefore the administration of justice has been done the way the law expects it to be done. So the conviction is certainly legitimate,” he said. Prof. Agyemang-Duah stressed that the conviction should not be treated as the conclusion of the fight against illegal mining, insisting that state institutions responsible for prosecution must pursue other alleged kingpins identified in various reports. “If, in fact, this has happened, and we know of many other kingpins in this particular activity, then I think it is important for government and the agencies responsible for prosecuting such people to make sure all those who have been identified in the many reports of Mr Asare Donkor and others begin to really bring these people to court,” he stated. He cautioned that failure to prosecute other alleged offenders could create perceptions that the fight against galamsey is politically motivated. “Because this should not end there. And if, in fact, it happens that only one person coming from the other political party is the one to be convicted, then of course people will have all kinds of reasons to raise questions whether this is a political kind of movement. But it’s not political,” he said. The governance expert maintained that the focus should remain on accountability and ensuring that anyone found culpable of destroying Ghana’s forests and water bodies faces the law, regardless of political affiliation.

items.title: UK must ‘regain our stability,’ says Andy Burnham after becoming seventh prime minister in decade
items.description: The United Kingdom enters a new chapter of leadership today as Andy Burnham prepares to assume office as Prime Minister. The 56-year-old politician will formalise his appointment on Monday following a traditional audience with King Charles III at Buckingham Palace. His ascent marks a significant shift for the governing Labour Party after the sudden resignation of Keir Starmer. A Familiar Transition of Power The political climate in London remains volatile. Burnham serves as the seventh Prime Minister in a decade and the fifth to hold the office in just four years. The transition follows intense instability within the Labour Party, which saw Starmer step down last month after a series of electoral defeats, policy U-turns, and internal scandals. Starmer holds the distinction of being the shortest-serving Labour prime minister in history. Because the United Kingdom’s parliamentary system permits governing parties to replace leaders mid-term, Burnham secures the premiership without a general election. He officially assumed the role of Labour Party leader this past Friday. Starmer is scheduled to deliver a final farewell address outside 10 Downing Street before travelling to the palace to formally tender his resignation. Upon his departure, the King will invite Burnham to form a new government in a constitutional tradition known as “kissing hands.” Promises of Radical Reform Upon returning to Downing Street, the incoming Prime Minister is expected to deliver a landmark speech outlining his national vision. Burnham, often dubbed the “King of the North,” has signalled a sharp departure from the status quo. In a speech delivered Friday, he vowed to reshape the country’s governing philosophy. “Let’s have the courage to fix the big things that politics has neglected,” Burnham stated, emphasising his intent to reverse structural changes initiated in the 1980s. He argued that the nation has suffered since the era when “political power was centralised, and economic power was privatised.” His stated mission is “to pass power from the authorities to ordinary people” and “to build a new politics — the country is crying out for it.” He intends to offer the British public “more breathing room” while championing a more “stable and responsible” style of governance. An Urgent Domestic Agenda Burnham inherits a government under severe socioeconomic pressure. His inbox contains systemic challenges, including a persistent cost-of-living crisis and the ongoing struggle to revive the ailing National Health Service. Beyond domestic infrastructure, he must manage contentious debates regarding immigration and the country’s post-Brexit relationship with Europe. Political survival will require balancing these domestic priorities against a surging opposition. The Reform UK party, led by Donald Trump ally Nigel Farage, currently leads in national opinion polls. While Burnham prepares to navigate these external threats, he already contends with internal friction. Cabinet formation has sparked immediate disagreement, highlighted by the resignation of Prisons Minister Lord Timpson earlier today. Reports suggest the new administration may explore an income tax cut to stimulate the economy, though precise details of his legislative agenda remain the subject of intense speculation in Westminster. Whether Burnham can bridge these deep-seated partisan divides and stabilise the economy remains the defining question of his premiership. As he moves from the campaign trail to the dispatch box, his ability to deliver tangible results—rather than just rhetoric—will determine if he can restore public trust or if the United Kingdom’s era of revolving-door leadership will continue.

items.title: Anéfis : la victoire est militaire, la guerre est ailleurs
items.description: © JDM Le 4 juillet 2026, à l'aube, une coalition du Groupe de soutien à l'islam et aux musulmans, affilié à Al-Qaïda, et du Front de libération de l'Azawad attaque simultanément Anéfis, Gao, Aguelhok, Sévaré et Kéniéroba. Les assaillants s'emparent de la ville d'Anéfis dans la journée. Mais le camp militaire, tenu par les Forces armées maliennes et le contingent russe d'Africa Corps, refuse de céder. Encerclé, bombardé, pilonné par des drones, il tient. Tout ce qui suit découle de ce refus, et il faut le dire sans réserve : c'est une victoire. Mais une victoire ne dispense pas de comprendre la guerre qu'elle vient d'interrompre. Et cette guerre ne se joue déjà plus là où nous la regardons. LA BATAILLE S'EST DÉCIDÉE SUR LA ROUTE Reprenons la séquence, telle qu'elle est établie par les correspondants de RFI sur place, par l'AFP et par les communiqués successifs de l'État-major général des armées. Une fois le siège installé, l'adversaire n'a pas cherché à emporter le camp de vive force. Il a reporté son effort sur l'axe venant de Gao, c'est-à-dire sur ce qui pouvait sauver la garnison. Un premier convoi de renfort est engagé le 5 juillet : il est stoppé dans la zone de Tabrichat. Un second part dans la nuit du 7 au 8 juillet, subit une attaque de grande intensité vers Tabankort le 9, et parvient à Anéfis dans la nuit du 9 au 10. Entre-temps, le 8, les soldats maliens sortent du camp et reprennent pied dans la ville, où ils se filment, drapeau à la main. La ville a changé de mains deux fois en moins d'une semaine. Ce n'est donc pas elle qui a décidé de l'issue. Ce qui a décidé, c'est le convoi : sa capacité à passer, et l'entêtement de l'adversaire à l'en empêcher. La bataille d'Anéfis s'est jouée sur les cent kilomètres de piste qui séparent Anéfis de Gao. Un fait mérite d'être relevé ici, parce qu'il ruine un récit. Ce convoi ne comptait pas seulement des soldats maliens et le contingent russe : des combattants touaregs du GATIA et du MSA y participaient. Des hommes du Nord se sont battus pour que le Nord reste malien. Cette guerre n'oppose pas Bamako aux Touaregs, et ceux qui le prétendent, à Kidal comme dans certaines rédactions étrangères, le savent parfaitement. CE QUE NOTRE ÉTAT-MAJOR A DÉSIGNÉ L'adversaire a donc fait de la route sa cible. Le plus remarquable est que notre état-major, de son côté, désigne le même centre de gravité. Invité du journal télévisé de l'ORTM, le chef d'état-major général des armées, le général de division Elysée Jean Dao, a présenté l'opération comme poursuivant deux objectifs : ravitailler les unités déployées à Anéfis, et détruire les principaux moyens de combat des groupes armés. Il a inscrit la bataille dans une stratégie de réduction des capacités adverses, appelée à se poursuivre sur l'ensemble du territoire. Ravitailler et détruire des moyens : ni l'un ni l'autre ne se mesure en kilomètres carrés. L'objet de cette guerre n'est pas le sol, c'est le matériel et son acheminement. Les assaillants frappent nos convois pour asphyxier nos garnisons ; nos forces détruisent leurs véhicules pour user leur capacité de manœuvre. Des deux bouts du champ de bataille, un même constat : ce n'est pas le terrain qui décide, c'est la logistique. On m'opposera Kidal, et l'objection mérite d'être traitée. Depuis avril 2026, l'adversaire tient la ville : il prend donc du territoire, et il le garde. Mais qu'en a-t-il fait ? Rien, sinon une base d'où partir vers autre chose. Il ne l'administre pas, il n'y élève pas d'État, il n'y produit rien. Une ville, entre ses mains, ne vaut pas comme territoire : elle vaut comme point d'appui pour couper des axes, et la preuve en est qu'il a marché sur Anéfis, c'est-à-dire sur le verrou de la route qui monte vers Kidal. Le territoire est son moyen ; le flux est sa fin. Reste à savoir de quoi le flux, lui, est le moyen. Car couper une route n'est pas davantage une fin en soi. C'est ici que la stratégie de l'adversaire apparaît dans son entier, et elle n'est pas militaire. LE MONOPOLE DE L'ORDINAIRE En septembre 2025, le JNIM interdit les importations de carburant vers le Mali et s'attaque systématiquement aux camions-citernes venant du Sénégal et de la Côte d'Ivoire. Des centaines de citernes sont incendiées. Dans un pays enclavé qui importe par la route la quasi-totalité de son carburant, l'effet est immédiat : files de trois jours devant les stations de Bamako, coupures d'électricité, moissons compromises, pénuries alimentaires. Et le 26 octobre, le ministre de l'Éducation nationale, Amadou Sy Savané, annonce à la télévision la suspension des cours dans toutes les écoles et universités du pays, du 27 octobre au 9 novembre : faute de carburant, les enseignants ne peuvent plus se déplacer. Il faut mesurer ce qui s'est produit ce jour-là. Le JNIM a fermé toutes les écoles du Mali sans entrer dans une seule salle de classe, sans tenir un seul quartier de Bamako, en brûlant des camions sur les routes de Dakar et d'Abidjan. Or fermer les écoles d'un pays n'est pas un acte terroriste : c'est un acte souverain, une prérogative d'État. Ce jour-là, deux autorités décidaient si les enfants maliens iraient en classe. Voilà la guerre qui nous est faite, et elle n'est ni une guerre de conquête ni même une guerre des routes. Les routes ne sont qu'un moyen. L'adversaire ne cherche pas à prendre le Mali : il cherche à priver l'État malien du monopole de l'ordinaire. Il n'attaque pas la souveraineté par le drapeau, il l'attaque par le quotidien. Le plein d'essence. Le camion qui livre. L'instituteur qui rejoint son poste. L'enfant qui va à l'école. Le calcul est limpide, et il n'a rien de militaire. Un État qui ne garantit plus le banal perd d'abord la confiance, puis la patience de son peuple. Ce que vise l'adversaire, c'est l'exaspération : la file d'attente, la rentrée annulée, le prix du mil qui monte. Il ne peut pas gagner une bataille rangée, Anéfis vient de le lui rappeler. Alors il travaille à rendre le pays ingouvernable assez longtemps pour que la lassitude fasse ce que ses armes ne peuvent pas faire : une instabilité durable, ou une rupture politique. D'où la formule qui décrit le plus exactement notre situation : un État dont chaque acte banal exige une opération militaire a déjà perdu une part de sa souveraineté, même s'il tient toutes ses villes. Car la souveraineté réelle n'est pas une couleur sur une carte : c'est la capacité de rendre la vie ordinaire à nouveau ordinaire. LA QUESTION QUI DÉCIDERA DE LA SUITE Si tel est l'objectif adverse, alors la question de l'après-Anéfis se pose en deux temps. Le premier est militaire, et il tient à la reconstitution. Une coalition qui aligne des blindés, des pièces d'artillerie, des drones et des centaines de véhicules ne fabrique rien de tout cela : elle le reçoit. Il existe donc, en amont de chaque véhicule détruit à Anéfis, une chaîne de financements, de sanctuaires, de corridors et de marchés d'armes. Détruire le stock est nécessaire ; tarir la source est ce qui rend la destruction décisive. Une stratégie d'usure sans stratégie d'interdiction de la reconstitution revient à vider une baignoire dont le robinet reste ouvert. Le second n'est pas militaire, et c'est le plus important : à quelle vitesse l'État reprend-il le monopole de l'ordinaire ? Cela se mesure, et cela ne figure dans aucun communiqué. Le prix, le délai et l'escorte nécessaires pour qu'un camion aille de Gao à Anéfis, ou d'Abidjan à Bamako. Le nombre d'écoles ouvertes, non pas décrétées ouvertes, mais où les maîtres arrivent et où les enfants viennent. Le nombre de marchés hebdomadaires qui se tiennent. Voilà les chiffres qui disent la souveraineté telle qu'elle se vit. Ce sont eux qu'il faudrait publier. CE QUE NOUS DEVONS À CEUX QUI ONT TENU Il faut, pour finir, revenir à ceux par qui tout cela tient. Plusieurs jours durant, des hommes ont défendu un camp encerclé, sous les drones et l'artillerie, face à un adversaire supérieur en nombre, sans savoir si le secours arriverait. D'autres ont pris la route pour eux, en connaissant les mines, les embuscades et les véhicules piégés qui les attendaient. Certains ne sont pas revenus. Le pays leur doit davantage qu'une image partagée : il leur doit la mémoire de leurs noms, le soin de leurs blessés, la dignité de leurs familles. Le courage d'Anéfis n'est pas un slogan. Il a un prix, et il a été payé. Mais ce sacrifice appelle une contrepartie, et cette contrepartie n'est pas militaire. Le soldat escorte le carburant ; il ne peut pas faire que le carburant n'ait plus besoin d'escorte. Il tient la route ; il ne peut pas la construire. Il reprend une localité ; il ne peut pas y rouvrir l'école, y installer un juge, y ramener un dispensaire. Il détruit les véhicules de l'ennemi ; il ne peut pas tarir les circuits qui les remplaceront. Voilà la part qui n'est pas la leur. Elle a des noms précis : l'État civil, qui doit revenir là où le drapeau tient ; les travaux publics et le budget, qui font les routes et les rendent praticables ; la diplomatie, qui négocie nos corridors d'importation avec nos voisins et qui doit assécher l'armement de l'ennemi à la source ; l'école, enfin, qui décide de ce que le Mali sera dans vingt ans. L'objectif national n'est donc ni une carte ni un communiqué. Il porte un nom que nul n'ose écrire parce qu'il paraît trop humble : la reconquête de la normalité. Nos soldats ont fait leur part, et bien au-delà. Il reste à savoir si le reste du Mali fera la sienne. Oumar Sidibé est docteur en relations internationales et auteur de plusieurs travaux sur le Sahel, la sécurité et la gouvernance.

items.title: Canada bars travelers from DR Congo over Ebola outbreak, defies WHO warnings
items.description: The Canadian government announced Sunday that it will temporarily bar foreign nationals who have visited the Democratic Republic of the Congo within the last 21 days. This new border measure aims to curb the potential spread of the worsening Ebola outbreak currently centred in the African nation. The restrictions are scheduled to take effect Monday, July 20, at 11:59 p.m. Government rationale for the border ban Federal officials maintain that these precautionary steps are necessary to protect the domestic population. In a formal statement released Sunday, the Public Health Agency of Canada explained that “limiting entry of foreign nationals who have been in [Congo] in the previous 21 days may reduce public health risks for Canadians.” The agency did not provide further comment regarding the enforcement logistics of the policy. Conflict with international health guidance The decision places Ottawa at direct odds with the World Health Organisation (WHO), which maintains that broad travel and trade restrictions are counterproductive during public health emergencies. The U.N. agency argues that such measures often fail to contain the virus while inadvertently fueling social stigma. “People from affected regions and African communities have faced unfair suspicion,” the WHO stated last month. The organisation further emphasised that “the spread of Ebola is not determined by nationality or ethnicity.” According to current WHO assessments, the overall risk of the Congolese outbreak spreading internationally remains low. Context of the current outbreak The Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a severe public health crisis as local authorities struggle to contain the virus. As of July 15, health officials reported more than 2,100 confirmed Ebola cases and 828 deaths. The disease is primarily transmitted through direct contact with the bodily fluids of an infected individual. Rising trend in Western travel restrictions Canada is the latest nation to implement localised travel hurdles in response to the crisis. Last week, the United States introduced similar mandates, preventing American citizens who have been in the Congo within the previous 21 days from returning home via commercial aviation. The impact of these global policies is already visible on the ground. Seven American aid workers, who travelled to the region specifically to assist in the containment effort, are currently isolated in a mandatory quarantine facility in Kenya before they can return to the United States. As Western nations tighten their borders, public health experts continue to weigh the balance between domestic risk mitigation and the humanitarian need for international support. Whether these unilateral restrictions serve as a necessary safeguard or an obstacle to global cooperation remains a point of contention as the international community works to suppress the outbreak at its source.

items.title: Burkina/Soins prénatals : Un atelier pour partager les résultats du projet de recherche de mise en œuvre sur les nouvelles directives nationales
items.description: L'Institut de recherche en sciences de la santé (IRSS), en collaboration avec la Direction de la santé de la famille et l'Organisation mondiale de la santé (OMS), a organisé ce lundi 20 juillet 2026 à Ouagadougou un atelier de restitution des résultats du projet de recherche de mise en œuvre sur les nouvelles directives nationales en matière de soins prénatals (NAMAI). Cette rencontre vise à partager les résultats de l'étude avec les acteurs du ministère de la Santé et les partenaires techniques et financiers afin de renforcer l'amélioration de la santé maternelle. Cette rencontre réunit les acteurs du ministère de la Santé ainsi que les partenaires techniques et financiers autour des principaux résultats du projet. Elle constitue un cadre d'échanges sur les acquis, les défis et les perspectives liés à la mise en œuvre des nouvelles recommandations nationales en matière de consultations prénatales. Le projet NAMAI a pour objectif d'évaluer l'acceptabilité et l'efficacité de la mise en œuvre de ces nouvelles directives. Il visait également à apprécier la valeur ajoutée perçue par les femmes enceintes et les prestataires de santé, à analyser la fidélité de la mise en œuvre ainsi que les coûts associés, tout en examinant les conditions nécessaires à sa durabilité et à son extension à plus grande échelle. Une vue des participants Mis en œuvre dans 16 formations sanitaires réparties dans huit districts sanitaires des régions du Guiriko, du Bankui et du Sourou, le projet s'est déroulé sur une période de cinq ans. Sa phase opérationnelle a couvert la période allant d'octobre 2019 à décembre 2024. À travers cette restitution, les organisateurs entendent mettre à la disposition des décideurs et des partenaires des données probantes susceptibles d'orienter les politiques et les stratégies en faveur de l'amélioration de la qualité des soins prénatals et, plus largement, de la santé maternelle et néonatale au Burkina Faso. « Ce projet est né parce que malgré la réduction de la mortalité maternelle, elle reste relativement élevée dans les pays africains. Donc, il est nécessaire d'agir au niveau de la qualité des soins offerts aux femmes. Dans ce cadre, l'OMS a mis en place de nouvelles recommandations. La consultation prénatale passe de quatre à huit avec des soins beaucoup plus améliorés. Il s'agit d'améliorer la qualité des soins pour permettre d'identifier et de prévenir tous les risques liés à la grossesse. Grâce au projet, aujourd'hui, au Burkina Faso, on est capable de mettre en œuvre cette nouvelle recommandation. Nous sommes capables d'améliorer les soins prénataux dans les services de premier contact que sont les Centres de santé et de promotion sociale (CSPS). Ça, c'est le premier élément. Le second élément, c'est que les femmes adhèrent. Elles reçoivent tous les soins dont elles ont besoin. Elles arrivent à faire l'échographie pour voir la vitalité du fœtus pour pouvoir faire la datation du fœtus. Ce sont des résultats qui sont à notre avis très positifs », a indiqué le coordonnateur du projet, Pr. Seni Kouanda. Pr Seni Kouanda, coordonnateur du projet Malgré les résultats satisfaisants du projet, il subsiste encore des défis. Il s'agit entre autres de ruptures de stocks, des ressources humaines qualifiées. « Il faut que dans les zones rurales, nous ayons du personnel qualifié pour pouvoir prendre en charge les femmes », a dit le Pr. Kouanda. Cette rencontre va permettre de faire des recommandations afin de trouver des solutions durables. Pour la représentante du secrétaire général du ministère de la Santé, Pr. Valérie Zombré, cette restitution est capitale au regard des défis persistants et des ambitions nationales. Selon lui, le pays envisage une réduction significative de la mortalité maternelle d'ici 2030. Par conséquent, les résultats issus de cette recherche doivent servir de levier pour accélérer la mise à l'échelle des interventions efficaces. Elle a invité les participants à des discussions franches et constructives afin de trouver des solutions durables. Pr Valérie Zombré, représentante du secrétaire général du ministère de la Santé Rama Diallo Lefaso.net

items.title: Comoé/Conseil consultatif de la jeunesse : Le PDS de Niangoloko sensibilise les jeunes sur sa mise en place
items.description: BURKINA-COMOE-JEUNESSE-CONSEIL Comoé/Conseil consultatif de la jeunesse : Le PDS de Niangoloko sensibilise les jeunes sur sa mise en place Niangoloko, 20 juil. 2026 (AIB) – Le Président de la délégation spéciale (PDS) de la commune de Niangoloko, Ousséni Ouédraogo, a animé, le vendredi 17 juillet 2026, une rencontre d'information et de sensibilisation avec les jeunes de la commune sur le processus de mise en place du Conseil national consultatif de la jeunesse du Burkina Faso (CNCJ-BF). La rencontre, tenue à la mairie de Niangoloko, a réuni les organisations, associations et mouvements de jeunesse, ainsi que de nombreux jeunes de la commune. Elle visait à présenter les objectifs et les missions du Conseil national consultatif de la jeunesse du Burkina Faso (CNCJ-BF). A cette occasion, l'arrêté interministériel fixant les modalités de désignation des membres des bureaux a été présenté et expliqué afin de permettre à l'ensemble des jeunes et des structures de jeunesse de disposer du même niveau d'information avant la mise en place du bureau communal de Niangoloko. Cette rencontre a également permis de recueillir les préoccupations et les propositions des jeunes en vue de renforcer leur participation à la gouvernance des politiques publiques en faveur de la jeunesse. Les échanges, francs et participatifs, ont permis aux participants d'exprimer leurs préoccupations et de solliciter des éclaircissements sur le processus. Le Président de la délégation spéciale les a invités à se mobiliser le moment venu afin de mettre en place un bureau dynamique, représentatif et capable de défendre les intérêts de la jeunesse de la commune. À l'issue de la rencontre, les participants se sont déclarés satisfaits des explications fournies sur l'arrêté interministériel et ont réaffirmé leur engagement à œuvrer pour les intérêts de la jeunesse de la commune urbaine de Niangoloko. Les membres du bureau communal de Niangoloko seront désignés dans les prochains jours. Agence d'information du Burkina BBH/no/yos

items.title: Sacre de la sélection espagnole au Mondial : les félicitations du Roi à Felipe VI
items.description: Le Roi Mohammed VI a adressé un message de félicitations au Roi Felipe VI d’Espagne à l’occasion du sacre de la sélection nationale espagnole de football qui a remporté la 23e édition de la Coupe du Monde, accueillie par les Etats-Unis d’Amérique, le Mexique et le Canada. Dans ce message, le Souverain exprime à Felipe VI, et à travers lui au peuple espagnol ami, ses chaleureuses félicitations à la suite de ce grand exploit sportif, qui vient confirmer la forte présence du football espagnol à l’échelle mondiale. « En partageant Moi-même et le peuple marocain, avec vous et le peuple espagnol, les sentiments de joie et de fierté pour ce deuxième titre mondial du genre remporté par votre pays voisin et ami, Je tiens à saluer la forte détermination et le haut esprit de compétitivité dont ont fait montre les composantes de votre jeune sélection nationale tout au long de ce championnat, à même de lui permettre de remporter avec brio et mérite la Coupe du Monde » , écrit le Roi. Réitérant au Souverain d’Espagne ses félicitations à la suite de ce titre footballistique mondial, le Roi souhaite davantage de rayonnement et de distinction au football espagnol. items.title: Wontumi taken to Nsawam Prison to begin 20-year sentence – Dr Boakye-Danquah confirms
items.description: Former Government Spokesperson on Governance and Security, Dr Palgrave Boakye-Danquah, has confirmed that Ashanti Regional Chairman of the New Patriotic Party (NPP), Bernard Antwi-Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, has been transferred to the Nsawam Medium Security Prison. This is to begin serving his 20-year prison sentence following his conviction over illegal mining activities. Speaking on Asempa FM’s Ekosiisen show on Monday, July 20, Dr Boakye-Danquah said he had just parted ways with the NPP regional chairman as prison authorities prepared to transport him to the correctional facility. “I have just parted ways with him, and he is being taken to Nsawam. How did we get to this stage as a country?” he said. His confirmation came hours after the Criminal Division of the Accra High Court sentenced Chairman Wontumi to 20 years’ imprisonment over illegal mining activities on the Samreboi concession in the Western Region. Justice Audrey Kocuvie-Tay, who presided over the case, also imposed a fine of 10,000 penalty units, equivalent to GH¢120,000. Should the fine remain unpaid, Chairman Wontumi faces an additional default sentence of three years’ imprisonment. Reacting to the court’s decision, Dr Boakye-Danquah said the defence team had not anticipated a conviction, maintaining that the evidence presented during the trial did not establish Chairman Wontumi’s personal involvement in illegal mining. “We did not expect this outcome because, based on the evidence we provided, it was clear that there was nothing presented in court that showed Chairman Wontumi was personally involved in illegal mining throughout the proceedings,” he stated. He indicated that the defence had remained optimistic throughout the trial that the evidence would ultimately favour their client. Dr Boakye-Danquah also disclosed that the defence had expected the trial court to take into account a pending application before the Supreme Court before delivering its judgement. According to him, the legal team believed the application warranted further consideration before the High Court concluded the matter. “We were therefore very hopeful that, considering the application that was filed at the Supreme Court, Her Ladyship would take her time to consider the matter. Unfortunately, she did not agree with our position and went ahead to deliver her judgement,” he said. The High Court, however, proceeded to deliver its ruling, convicting and sentencing Chairman Wontumi. Dr Boakye-Danquah described the outcome as deeply disappointing and expressed concern that the conviction could be perceived as political victimisation because of Chairman Wontumi’s status within the opposition New Patriotic Party. “It is very sad. It feels like a form of political victimisation of a politically exposed individual,” he remarked. He emphasised that Chairman Wontumi occupies one of the most influential positions within the NPP. “We are not talking about just any other person. We are talking about the sitting Ashanti Regional Chairman of the New Patriotic Party, the stronghold of the party and the largest opposition party.” Dr Boakye-Danquah’s comments reflect his personal assessment of the case. The High Court convicted Chairman Wontumi following criminal proceedings relating to illegal mining activities. The former government spokesperson further questioned whether authorities would pursue other individuals alleged to be involved in illegal mining with the same level of determination. “If this is the direction we are taking, while we know that there are others who are actively involved in galamsey, then we will wait and see how things unfold,” he said. He added that, despite the disappointment surrounding the verdict, he hoped the country would remain united. “We can only hope and pray that our country can come together and find its way forward.” Dr Boakye-Danquah defended Chairman Wontumi’s conduct throughout the trial, saying the NPP regional chairman had consistently respected the judicial process. According to him, Chairman Wontumi honoured every court appearance and cooperated fully with the proceedings from the outset. “Chairman Wontumi has been law-abiding throughout this case. There was not a single day that he failed to appear in court. We have been very cooperative with the court and followed due process,” he said. He suggested that the accused’s compliance demonstrated his willingness to submit himself to the authority of the courts while the case progressed.
