items.title: Wife’s right to dower remains protected if marriage dissolved due to husband’s misconduct: LHC
items.description: LAHORE: The Lahore High Court (LHC) has ruled that a wife’s right to her dower ( Haq Mehr ) remains protected if a marriage is dissolved due to the husband’s cruelty or misconduct, even if the family court formally terms the decree as “ Khula ”. Dismissing a petition filed by a husband challenging a family court decision that awarded 50 per cent of the deferred dower to his former wife, Justice Mohsin Akhtar Kayani issued directives to end the automatic forfeiture of dower in marital dissolution cases. According to details, the couple married on March 18, 2022, with a deferred dower of Rs100,000. Shortly afterwards, the wife filed a suit for dissolution of marriage, alleging that her husband subjected her to physical and verbal abuse when she demanded payment of the dower. She alleged that her husband remained unemployed, was involved in theft and intoxication, and forcibly turned her out of the house. A senior civil judge (family division) in Toba Tek Singh dissolved the marriage and awarded 50 per cent of the deferred dower to the wife under Section 10(4) of the Family Courts Act, 1964. The husband challenged the dower award, arguing that after the Federal Shariat Court (FSC) struck down sub-sections (5) and (6) of Section 10 in 2022, family courts no longer had the authority to grant any part of the dower upon dissolution of a marriage. However, Justice Kayani rejected the husband’s argument, clarifying the critical legal distinction between a divorce sought purely due to a wife’s personal aversion ( Khula simpliciter) and a dissolution caused by the husband’s misconduct ( Nashuz ). The judge noted that under Sections 2 and 5 of the Dissolution of Muslim Marriages Act (DMMA), 1939, if a wife proves statutory grounds such as physical, psychological, verbal or economic abuse, her dower is neither affected, reduced nor forfeited — whether prompt or deferred, paid or unpaid. The judge added that cruelty was a civil matter to be decided on the balance of probabilities. “A wife’s credible testimony does not require medical certificates or police reports to be acted upon by a family court,” Justice Kayani ruled. He also observed that the family courts cannot routinely convert a suit founded on cruelty into a simple Khula decree without the wife’s explicit informed consent. The judge maintained that an unpaid deferred dower was a debt owed by the husband and cannot be erased under the guise of Khula . “Dower is a proprietary right arising from the contract of marriage. It is not a penalty upon the husband, nor a benefit held at the Family Court’s discretion. It cannot be forfeited, reduced or apportioned without a specific finding that the law permits it,” Justice Kayani ruled. The judge further observed that the right to dissolve a marriage and the right to dower were separate rights. “A decree of dissolution does not by itself decide the fate of the dower that requires its own finding, on its own evidence,” he added. Justice Kayani highlighted that much of the confusion in trial courts arose because sub-sections (5) and (6) of Section 10 were struck down by the FSC without new legislative provisions replacing them, leading family courts to mistakenly deny women their dower across the board. The judge further observed that there was a need for a fresh provision, drafted in the language of Section 5 of the Dissolution of Muslim Marriages Act, 1939, requiring family courts to independently record a finding on the cause of dissolution before deciding the question of dower, and making it clear that dower is not affected where a ground under Section 2 of that Act is proved. The judge directed the LHC registrar to forward copies of the judgment to the Punjab Law and Parliamentary Affairs Department and the Law and Justice Commission of Pakistan for drafting fresh legislation explicitly protecting dower rights in dissolution cases.

La Chine annonce la création d’une coalition internationale sur l’intelligence artificielle réunissant 29 pays, dont la Russie
items.description: Le president chinois Xi Jinping a annonce, le 17 juillet a Shanghai, la creation d'une coalition internationale consacree a l'intelligence artificielle. La ceremonie de signature de l'accord instituant cette nouvelle organisation s'etait tenue la veille. Au total, 29 etats participent a l'initiative, parmi lesquels la Chine, la Russie, la Bielorussie, le Bresil, le Kazakhstan et l'Indonesie. La coalition reunit egalement des pays membres des BRICS, de l'Organisation de cooperation de Shanghai et de l'Association des nations de l'Asie du Sud-Est, ainsi que plusieurs etats d'Afrique, d'Asie, du monde arabe et d'Amerique latine. Elle doit rester ouverte a de nouvelles adhesions. L'annonce a ete faite lors de la Conference mondiale sur l'intelligence artificielle, connue sous le sigle WAIC, l'un des principaux rendez-vous chinois consacres a ce secteur. Xi Jinping y intervenait personnellement pour la premiere fois. Baptisee Organisation mondiale de cooperation dans le domaine de l'intelligence artificielle, la nouvelle structure doit avoir son siege a Shanghai. Elle doit fournir un cadre commun pour le developpement de technologies, l'elaboration de regles internationales et la definition de normes de securite. Une intelligence artificielle ouverte et sous controle humain Dans son discours, Xi Jinping a appele les pays a renforcer leur cooperation et a favoriser un developpement plus ouvert des technologies. « Une seule corde ne peut produire de musique et un seul arbre ne peut former une foret. Le developpement de l'intelligence artificielle doit devenir une symphonie de cooperation internationale », a declare le dirigeant chinois. Cette ouverture doit egalement s'accompagner de garanties en matiere de securite. Xi Jinping a insiste sur la necessite de maintenir les systemes d'intelligence artificielle sous controle humain. Il a demande la mise en place de lois, de mecanismes de surveillance technologique, de systemes d'alerte precoce et de procedures de reaction rapide afin de prevenir les abus et les utilisations malveillantes. Pekin entend developper la cooperation internationale dans un secteur devenu central pour l'economie, l'industrie et la souverainete technologique. Cette volonte doit se traduire par des mesures concretes en faveur des pays en developpement. Au cours des cinq prochaines annees, la Chine prevoit de proposer 5 000 possibilites de participation a des programmes de formation consacres a l'intelligence artificielle. Des centres internationaux destines a l'application de ces technologies doivent egalement voir le jour. Un nouveau levier de cooperation pour la Russie et la Chine Pour la Russie, cette initiative constitue un nouveau cadre de cooperation technologique avec la Chine. Le premier vice-president du directoire de Sberbank, Alexandre Vediakhine, a souligne que Moscou et Pekin partageaient un meme objectif de souverainete dans le domaine de l'intelligence artificielle. Il a mis en avant l'experience acquise par la Chine dans le developpement de modeles d'IA et dans leur integration a l'industrie comme a l'administration publique. La WAIC permet notamment a la Russie de presenter ses propres solutions, de renforcer ses partenariats avec la Chine et de preparer de nouveaux projets communs. La creation de cette organisation intervient sur fond de restrictions americaines visant l'exportation vers la Chine de certains semi-conducteurs et de technologies avancees. Dans ce contexte, la coalition portee par Pekin offre a la Russie et a ses partenaires un espace supplementaire pour developper des solutions communes et renforcer leur souverainete technologique. Elle doit egalement leur permettre de reduire progressivement leur dependance a l'egard des technologies occidentales, tout en consolidant les echanges entre les pays qui souhaitent disposer de leurs propres capacites dans le domaine de l'intelligence artificielle.

items.title: Iran’s Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni arrives in Pakistan
items.description: Iran’s Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni on Monday night arrived in Islamabad on an official visit. Upon his arrival in Pakistan, the senior Iranian official was received by Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi. During his visit, Momeni will meet Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Chief of Defence Forces and Chief of the Army Staff Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, reported state-run Pakistan TV . Earlier reports had suggested that Momeni was likely to engage with Pakistani officials who previously mediated a now-collapsed interim ceasefire between the US and Iran. Momeni’s visit comes as mediator Pakistan continues its diplomatic efforts to rescue the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed last month between the US and Iran. In the ninth consecutive night of attacks on Monday, Washington expanded its strikes across Iran, including a city hosting a nuclear power plant. Iran kept up its own attacks across the region in response, with its Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) announcing they had hit targets in Bahrain, Jordan and Syria, while Bahrain said Tehran had targeted its air navigation systems. After US President Donald Trump’s remarks earlier this month that the ceasefire was “ over ”, his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian also said on Monday that they were fighting a “ full-scale war ”. Tehran has blamed Washington for breaching the MoU. Despite the latest attacks, Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said diplomatic exchanges with the US via mediators were ongoing. The Middle East war resumed after a weeks-long lull prompted by an agreement signed in June, and oil prices surged to a one-month high as the foes battled to control the vital Strait of Hormuz. As its truce with the US collapsed, Tehran reimposed its blockade of the waterway, while Washington hit back by again blockading Iran’s ports. Pakistan has been leading the peace efforts since the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran on February 28, setting off a war and global fuel crisis as Tehran took measures to disrupt traffic in the Strait of Hormuz — a vital maritime corridor for the supply of oil and gas. The Pakistan-mediated Islamabad MoU — a 14-point interim peace deal aimed at ending the war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz — was signed between the US and Iran on June 18.

items.title: 3e session ordinaire de Wawa 1 : Les élus locaux examinent le budget supplémentaire
items.description: Badou, 20 juil. (ATOP) – Le conseil municipal de Wawa 1 a ouvert, le lundi 20 juillet à Badou, les travaux de sa troisième session ordinaire de l’année. Les travaux de cette session seront principalement axés sur l’examen et l’adoption du budget supplémentaire exercice 2026, ainsi que le suivi de l’état d’avancement du projet de Financement des collectivités locales (FICOL). Cette session de six jours offre une opportunité privilégiée aux élus locaux de dresser le bilan à mi-parcours de leurs activités. Elle vise également à ajuster la trajectoire financière de la municipalité afin de garantir une gouvernance locale rigoureuse et transparente. Vue partielle des autorités à l’ouverture de la session Les travaux permettront aux conseillers municipaux de prendre connaissance des comptes-rendus des récentes missions menées par l’exécutif, d’amender les rapports de la session précédente et d’examiner le bilan moral et financier de la commune. L’agenda prévoit en outre une évaluation sur le terrain des réalisations du projet FICOL, suivie de la formulation de recommandations stratégiques pour ses phases ultérieures. Le maire de Wawa 1, Tchamdja Bawubadi Malaki, a exhorté ses pairs à mener des débats constructifs au service du bien-être des populations. Il a insisté sur l’importance du budget supplémentaire puis salué la politique nationale de développement des collectivités territoriales impulsée par le Président du Conseil, Faure Gnassingbé. Le préfet de Wawa, Soménu Atsu Yinassè a invité les conseillers à des échanges fructueux afin de répondre aux attentes des citoyens dans les domaines majeurs tels que la santé, l’assainissement, l’accès à l’eau potable, l’électrification et des infrastructures sociales. Il a encouragé les acteurs locaux à convenir de solutions pérennes en vue d’assurer la mise en service effective et imminente du marché moderne de Badou. ATOP/PM/KKT/BA

items.title: Mexican drug lord ‘El Mayo’ sentenced to life in prison in New York
items.description: Mexican drug lord Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada was sentenced Monday to life in prison by a federal court in New York for co-founding and leading the powerful Sinaloa cartel. The 76-year-old “El Mayo” is the former top associate of the organisation’s founder, Joaquin “El Chapo” Guzman, who is serving a life sentence in a maximum-security prison in Colorado. El Mayo was arrested at a US airport in July 2024 and pleaded guilty in August 2025 to several charges related to his leadership of the criminal organisation. He claims he was tricked by a cartel ally into boarding a plane that delivered him into the hands of US authorities. In exchange for his guilty plea, US authorities waived their right to seek the death penalty. A Justice Department statement said US District Judge Brian Cogan sentenced him to life in prison in the federal court in Brooklyn without the possibility of parole for his role as a principal leader of a “continuing criminal enterprise” and for racketeering violations. “Ismael Zambada Garcia spent nearly four decades poisoning American communities to make billions of dollars in profit and ordering the murders of anyone who stood in his way. Today, that chapter closes for good,” US Attorney for the Eastern District of New York Joseph Nocella, Jr. was quoted as saying. Known for keeping a low profile and avoiding being photographed, Zambada spent decades rising through the cartel ranks, evading both capture and multiple assassination attempts. The post Mexican drug lord ‘El Mayo’ sentenced to life in prison in New York appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Wontumi conviction ‘sad’ – Dr Boakye-Danquah questions political implications
items.description: Former Government Spokesperson on Governance and Security, Dr Palgrave Boakye-Danquah, has expressed disappointment over the court’s decision to convict and sentence New Patriotic Party (NPP) Ashanti Regional Chairman, Bernard Antwi-Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, over the Samreboi illegal mining case. His comments come after the Accra High Court sentenced the Ashanti Regional Chairman to 20 years’ imprisonment over illegal mining activities on the Samreboi concession in the Western Region. Justice Audrey Kocuvie-Tay, who presided over the Criminal Division of the High Court, delivered the sentence on Monday, July 20. The court also imposed a fine of 10,000 penalty units, equivalent to GH¢120,000, with a default sentence of three years’ imprisonment if the fine is not paid. Dr Boakye-Danquah, speaking on Asempa FM’s Ekosiisen show, said the outcome was unexpected because, in his view, the evidence presented by the defence team did not establish that Chairman Wontumi was personally involved in illegal mining activities. READ ALSO: Chairman Wontumi sentenced to 20 years in prison “We did not expect this outcome because, based on the evidence we provided, it was clear that there was nothing presented in court that showed Chairman Wontumi was personally involved in illegal mining throughout the proceedings,” he said. According to Dr Boakye-Danquah, the defence had been optimistic that an application filed at the Supreme Court would influence the court’s consideration of the matter before judgment was delivered. “We were therefore very hopeful that, considering the application that was filed at the Supreme Court, Her Ladyship would take her time to consider the matter. Unfortunately, she did not agree with our position and went ahead to deliver her judgment,” he stated. The former government spokesperson described the verdict as disappointing and suggested that the case could be viewed within the context of political victimisation. “It is very sad. It feels like a form of political victimisation of a politically exposed individual,” he said. Dr Boakye-Danquah stressed that Chairman Wontumi was not an ordinary individual but a prominent political figure whose position carries significant importance within the NPP. “We are not talking about just any other person; we are talking about the sitting Ashanti Regional Chairman of the New Patriotic Party, the stronghold of the party and the largest opposition party,” he added. While expressing disappointment with the judgment, Dr Boakye-Danquah questioned whether similar legal action would be taken against other individuals alleged to have links to illegal mining activities. “If this is the direction we are taking, while we know that there are others who are actively involved in galamsey, then we will wait and see how things unfold,” he said. He called for fairness and consistency in the fight against illegal mining, adding that the country must ensure that accountability applies equally to all individuals regardless of their political affiliation or status. “We can only hope and pray that our country can come together and find its way forward,” he said. Dr Boakye-Danquah also defended Chairman Wontumi’s conduct throughout the legal proceedings, describing him as cooperative and respectful of the judicial process. He said the NPP chairman had consistently appeared before the court and complied with all requirements during the trial. “Chairman Wontumi has been law-abiding throughout this case. There was not a single day that he failed to appear in court. We have been very cooperative with the court and followed due process,” he said. He revealed that he had recently met with Chairman Wontumi before the former was taken into custody following the court’s decision. “I have just parted ways with him, and he is being taken to Nsawam. How did we get to this stage in this country?” he questioned. The party’s leadership, including its flagbearer and legal team, has indicated that it is reviewing the judgment before announcing its next course of action.

items.title: AMA unveils 2 new compactor trucks to boost waste collection
items.description: The Accra Metropolitan Assembly (AMA) has unveiled two new refuse compactor trucks to boost waste collection, reduce the accumulation of refuse and improve sanitation services across the capital. The six-wheel-drive, rear-loading trucks were acquired at a total cost of about GH¢2.4 million and commissioned by the Minister for Local Government, Chieftaincy and Religious Affairs, Ahmed Ibrahim, at a ceremony held at the AMA headquarters in Accra. Each truck has a carrying capacity of about 20 cubic metres, a compaction ratio of between 3:1 and 4:1, and a payload capacity of between 9,500 and 10,000 kilogrammes. They are fitted with manual, electric and wireless remote-control operating systems to improve efficiency in waste collection. Speaking at the commissioning ceremony, Mr Ibrahim commended the Mayor, Assembly Members, directors and staff of the AMA for improving service delivery despite inheriting significant financial and legal challenges. He said the Assembly had previously struggled with garnished accounts, judgement debts, unpaid legal fees and several court cases, but had gradually stabilised its finances and strengthened its ability to undertake development projects. While describing the acquisition as a step in the right direction, the Minister said two trucks alone would not be enough to meet the sanitation demands of the capital. He urged the Assembly to work towards acquiring at least five or six refuse compactors to improve waste collection across the metropolis. Mr Ibrahim said that effective waste management requires strong collaboration between central government, metropolitan assemblies and private waste-management companies, noting that neither the public nor the private sector could tackle the challenge alone. He also called on neighbouring municipal assemblies to invest in their own sanitation equipment instead of depending heavily on the AMA to collect waste generated within their jurisdictions. According to him, Accra’s position as the country’s commercial hub means the Assembly manages refuse generated not only by residents but also by traders, commuters and visitors from across Greater Accra and other parts of the country. The Minister assured the Assembly that the Ministry would continue exploring ways to support the AMA because of the unique sanitation burden it bears as the authority responsible for the capital’s central business district. He also appealed to residents to help keep the city clean by reporting indiscriminate dumping of refuse into drains, on roads and at unauthorised locations through the Assembly’s hotline, 0544 914 628, to enable prompt enforcement action. The Mayor of Accra, Michael Kpakpo Allotey, expressed appreciation to President John Dramani Mahama for making funds available to support the Assembly’s sanitation and development programmes. He disclosed that the AMA did not own a single refuse compactor when he assumed office and had relied largely on rented equipment and services from private waste-management companies. According to the Mayor, the continuous rental of sanitation equipment was expensive and unsustainable, making it necessary for the Assembly to begin building its own fleet. Mr Allotey also said that sanitation management should not be limited by administrative boundaries, saying all metropolitan, municipal and district assemblies share responsibility for keeping the capital clean. He acknowledged public concerns over the poor maintenance of government-owned equipment and disclosed that the Assembly is taking steps to establish its own mechanical workshop to maintain its growing fleet of sanitation vehicles and equipment.

items.title: Long way to go in prosecuting those behind environmental destruction – Erastus Asare Donkor
items.description: Investigative journalist Erastus Asare Donkor says Ghana still has a long way to go in holding accountable individuals and companies responsible for severe environmental destruction, despite the conviction of New Patriotic Party (NPP) Ashanti Regional Chairman Bernard Antwi-Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi. Speaking on JoyNews’ The Pulse on Monday, July 20, Mr Asare Donkor said the conviction should not be viewed as the end of the fight against illegal mining, but rather a step towards ensuring that more people who have contributed to the destruction of Ghana’s natural resources face justice. According to him, the issue is not about celebrating the imprisonment of an individual but ensuring that the rule of law prevails. “It is not about being happy for the incarceration or the taking away of somebody’s freedom, but it is about the rule of law taking its course,” he said. Mr Asare Donkor said his investigative reports on illegal mining activities in the Samreboi area and the destruction of the Tano River have highlighted the environmental damage caused by mining operations linked to Akonta Mining Limited. He explained that since late 2021, he and his team have produced several reports, including the documentary " rel="nofollow"> Destruction for Gold , which captured evidence of illegal mining activities and their impact on the Tano Forest Reserve and the Tano River. He recalled that during investigations, some Chinese nationals mining along the Tano River referred them to Akonta Mining Limited when questioned about their licences, an indication of the company’s alleged involvement in activities in the area. Mr Asare Donkor said despite the reports and evidence gathered, authorities at the time failed to take decisive action to halt the destruction. “We kept highlighting them. Did the government pay heed to us? No. Back then, we had no response from government on the way forward,” he said. He argued that relevant state agencies could have intervened earlier by stopping the mining activities, imposing sanctions or revoking licences where necessary. The journalist, however, stressed that the case of Akonta Mining and Chairman Wontumi is only one example of a much broader environmental crisis. He cited the destruction of the Pamprama Forest Reserve, where he said Heritage Imperial Limited carried out mining activities before obtaining a licence, leaving the state to spend taxpayers’ money on reclamation efforts. “We have a long way to go concerning the list of people who have done extreme harm or committed nature crimes to this country, who need to answer for this before we can clamp down on the impunity that is going on,” he stated. Mr Asare Donkor called for sustained investigations and prosecution of individuals and companies whose activities have contributed to the degradation of Ghana’s forests and water bodies. His comments follow the Accra High Court’s conviction and sentencing of Chairman Wontumi to 20 years’ imprisonment over illegal mining activities on the Samreboi concession in the Western Region

items.title: Création annoncée d’un nouveau parti : le SEME de Serigne Gueye Diop répond à l’appel de Bassirou Diomaye Faye
items.description: Le parti Sénégal en mouvement vers l'excellence (Seme), dirigé par le Dr Serigne Gueye Diop, a annoncé son adhésion au projet de création d'un nouveau parti politique porté par le président de la République, Bassirou Diomaye Faye. Dans un manifeste publié lundi, le président du Seme, également ministre de l'Industrie et du Commerce, affirme que le chef de l'État a décidé de mettre en place une nouvelle formation politique « capable de l'accompagner dans son œuvre de refondation de la Nation sénégalaise ». Le SEME dit « saluer fortement » cette initiative, qu'il juge « pertinente » et « de haute portée stratégique ». Selon le document, cette future formation devra constituer un cadre de dialogue citoyen, de partage des valeurs et d'accompagnement de la vision politique du président de la République. Le parti de Dr Serigne Gueye Diop estime que cette nouvelle entité reposera sur des principes de souveraineté, de panafricanisme, d'État de droit et de transformation économique fondée sur la valorisation des ressources nationales. À ce titre, le Seme annonce sa participation à « l'acte fondateur » de cette future formation politique, présentée comme un outil destiné à accompagner les réformes engagées par le chef de l'État. Le manifeste ne précise toutefois ni le nom, ni la date de lancement, ni les modalités de création de ce nouveau parti.

items.title: Lagos police arrest 147 suspects, recover weapons, cartridges in criminal hideout raids
items.description: By Esther Onyegbula The Lagos State Police Command has arrested 147 suspects and recovered various weapons, including 250 expanded cartridges, 22 machetes, battle axes, knives, charms and substances suspected to be Indian hemp during raids on criminal hideouts across the state. The operation, carried out under the ongoing Operation KO S’AYE III, was conducted at about 5:30 a.m. on Sunday, July 19, 2026, by a joint team of police operatives from different formations of the command. The operatives, acting on credible intelligence, raided suspected criminal hideouts at Boolar, Super Ile-Epo and other identified black spots in the area as part of efforts to clamp down on hoodlums and criminal elements allegedly terrorising residents. In a statement on Monday, the Lagos State Police Public Relations Officer, SP Abimbola Adebisi, said the operation led to the arrest of 147 suspects and the recovery of several exhibits. Items recovered during the raids included 250 expanded cartridges, five battle axes, 22 machetes, two knives, one pair of scissors, one lighter, assorted charms and substances suspected to be Indian hemp. The command said the suspects were currently in police custody, adding that investigations were ongoing to establish their individual involvement in criminal activities. “Those found to have committed criminal offences will be prosecuted accordingly,” the statement added. The Commissioner of Police, Lagos State Command, CP Tijani Fatai, commended the officers involved in the operation for their professionalism and commitment to duty. Fatai assured residents that Operation KO S’AYE III would be sustained across the state to deny criminals safe havens and strengthen security. He also urged members of the public to continue providing timely and credible information to security agencies to assist in identifying criminal hideouts, apprehending offenders and improving safety across Lagos State. The post Lagos police arrest 147 suspects, recover weapons, cartridges in criminal hideout raids appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Insécurité mortifère (Le billet de Omar DIOUF)
items.description: Attaques à main armée, braquage de commerces, de domiciles, lynchages, vols à l’arraché, trafic de drogues, viols…. Dakar et sa banlieue demeurent sous la coupe des agresseurs à bord de moto, qui ont repris de plus belle leurs razzias nocturnes. Récemment dans les régions de Louga, Thiès, Diourbel, ainsi qu’à Diamniadio, à la Médina, en passant par Guédiawaye, les malfaiteurs ont refait parler d’eux avec une série de braquage de maisons, de commerces et même de meurtres, plongeant ainsi les populations dans l’inquiétude. Et si ce sont des agressions, ce sont de jeunes gens arrêtés dans de petits deals de drogues et autres produits prohibés. L’insécurité est toujours à nos portes et l’on ne sait plus à qui faire confiance ni à quel saint se vouer. Quand l’angoisse mortifère nous tient vraiment à la gorge… omar.diouf@lesoleil.sn

items.title: 2027: Adeleke risks reelection defeat over Tinubu support, Olawepo-Hashim warns
items.description: By Demola Akinyemi, Ilorin The presidential candidate of the Accord Party, Dr. Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim, has warned that Osun State Governor, Ademola Adeleke, risks losing his reelection bid in 2026 if he continues to support President Bola Tinubu at the expense of the party’s political interests. Olawepo-Hashim, through his support group, the Gbenga Hashim Solidarity Movement (GHSM), issued the warning while reacting to comments attributed to the Osun State Chairman of the Accord Party, Pastor Victor Akande, who reportedly questioned the relevance of the presidential candidate within the party. In a statement issued on Monday by the South-West Coordinator of GHSM, Alhaji Abass Olaniyi, the group described the comments credited to the party chairman as misleading, alleging that they were based on distorted facts and misrepresentation of political developments. GHSM said Governor Adeleke’s endorsement of President Tinubu was made before his movement into the Accord Party and should not be presented as a reflection of the party’s national position. According to the group, the Osun PDP leadership caucus endorsed President Tinubu for a second term in July 2025, while Adeleke was still a member of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). The group said it rejected the endorsement at the time because it was inconsistent with its political position. GHSM further stated that the national leadership of Accord Party later engaged Olawepo-Hashim in March 2026 as part of efforts to build a broader political platform ahead of the 2027 presidential election. It said the engagement, led by the party’s National Chairman, Barrister Maxwell Mgbudem, alongside other national officers, resulted in Olawepo-Hashim participating in the party’s presidential process and emerging as its candidate through direct primaries. The group argued that the development invalidated claims that Accord Party lacked a presidential direction when Adeleke endorsed President Tinubu. “The facts are clear. Governor Adeleke endorsed President Tinubu before joining Accord Party. Also, Accord Party’s national leadership invited Dr. Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim to provide presidential leadership for the party, and he emerged as the presidential candidate through open direct primaries,” GHSM stated. The group warned that Governor Adeleke’s approach within Accord Party could create internal contradictions similar to those that affected the PDP, stressing that political parties could only thrive when members respect party structures and collective decisions. “Accord Party must not travel the same path that weakened PDP. A political party cannot survive when individual calculations are placed above the collective aspirations of its members,” Abass said. GHSM also claimed that 24 state chairmen of the Accord Party had endorsed Olawepo-Hashim, while noting that the legal dispute surrounding the party’s presidential primary is currently before the Federal High Court in Abuja. The group said it would refrain from making public comments on the matter because it was already before the court. It urged Accord Party members to remain united, insisting that the strength of Governor Adeleke’s political future would depend on the cohesion of the platform under which he seeks reelection. GHSM reaffirmed its support for Olawepo-Hashim’s presidential ambition, describing the movement as one committed to political renewal, competence, institutional reform and national transformation. The post 2027: Adeleke risks reelection defeat over Tinubu support, Olawepo-Hashim warns appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Your Password Could Be The Weakest Link: 5 Mistakes That Put Your Accounts at Risk
items.description: Kennedy Bentum Jnr is a cybersecurity professional and holds a Master of Science in Forensic Accounting from the University of New Haven. (United States of America). He is CompTIA Security+ certified and writes about cybersecurity, cybercrime, fraud prevention, and digital safety. Imagine waking up one morning only to discover that you can no longer access your email account. Within minutes, you realise your social media accounts have also been taken over. Friends begin calling to ask why you’re asking them for money through WhatsApp. Before the day ends, your online banking profile has been locked because of suspicious login attempts. It sounds like the plot of a movie, but for many people, this is exactly how cybercrime begins, not with sophisticated hacking, but with a compromised password. In an age where our lives are increasingly connected to the internet, passwords have become the digital keys to our identities. They protect our bank accounts, emails, cloud storage, social media profiles, and even government services. Yet despite their importance, many people continue to underestimate the role passwords play in keeping them safe online. The uncomfortable truth is that cybercriminals don’t always need to break into a system. Sometimes, they simply log in using a password that was easy to guess, stolen through deception, or reused across multiple accounts. Why Passwords Still Matter With technologies such as facial recognition and fingerprint authentication becoming common on smartphones, it’s easy to assume that passwords are becoming less important. They are not. Biometric features often unlock your device, but behind the scenes, passwords remain the primary method of securing most online accounts. Whether you are accessing your email, online banking, work systems, or shopping platforms, your password is still one of the first barriers protecting your information. If that barrier is weak, everything behind it becomes vulnerable. Five Password Mistakes That Put You at Risk Using the Same Password Everywhere One of the biggest mistakes people make is reusing the same password across multiple accounts. Imagine using the same key for your house, office, car, and safe. If someone gets hold of that key, they gain access to everything. The same principle applies online. If one website suffers a data breach and your password is exposed, cybercriminals may try the same password on your email, social media, and banking accounts. Choosing Predictable Passwords Passwords such as 123456, password, qwerty, or even your date of birth may be easy to remember, but they are also among the first combinations attackers attempt. Many people also use the names of their children, favourite football clubs, or phone numbers. While these choices feel personal, they are often easy to discover through social media or public information. A stronger alternative is a long passphrase made up of unrelated words that is easy for you to remember but difficult for others to guess. Ignoring Multi-Factor Authentication Even strong passwords can be stolen through phishing attacks or data breaches. This is why enabling multi-factor authentication (MFA) is one of the most effective security measures available today. MFA requires a second form of verification—such as a code sent to your phone or generated by an authenticator app—before access is granted. Think of it as adding a second lock to your front door. Even if someone has your key, they still cannot get inside without the second layer of protection. Sharing Your Password Sharing passwords with friends, relatives, or colleagues may seem harmless, especially when done out of convenience. However, every additional person who knows your password increases the risk that it could be exposed intentionally or accidentally. Passwords should remain private, regardless of how much you trust the other person. Never Changing Compromised Passwords If a company announces that it has experienced a data breach, many users assume it has nothing to do with them. In reality, if your account was affected, your password may already be circulating among cybercriminals. Whenever you learn that one of your online accounts has been compromised, change your password immediately, especially if you have reused it elsewhere. How Cybercriminals Steal Passwords Contrary to popular belief, most attackers do not spend hours trying to guess passwords one character at a time. Instead, they rely on techniques such as phishing emails, fake login pages, malicious software that records keystrokes, and databases of passwords leaked during previous breaches. They also use credential stuffing, an automated method of testing stolen usernames and passwords across multiple websites in the hope that people have reused the same credentials. In many cases, the attacker succeeds because the victim unknowingly made the job easier. Protecting Yourself Starts with Simple Habits Improving your online security does not require advanced technical knowledge. A few simple habits can significantly reduce your risk. Create a unique password for every important account. Use long passphrases instead of short words. Enable multi-factor authentication wherever it is available. Consider using a reputable password manager to generate and store complex passwords securely. Most importantly, think twice before clicking on unexpected links or entering your login details on unfamiliar websites. Final Thoughts Cybersecurity often seems like a complicated subject reserved for technology experts, but it begins with everyday decisions that every internet user can make. Your password is more than just a way to log in. It is the first line of defence protecting your identity, your finances, and your personal information. The next time you create a password, ask yourself one simple question: If someone tried to steal my digital life today, would this password stop them? That answer could make all the difference.

items.title: Lacs/campagne de distribution des MII-2026 : Le comité local d’organisation évalue le bilan à mi-parcours
items.description: Aného, 20 juil. (ATOP) – Les membres du Comité local d’organisation (CLO) de la campagne de distribution gratuite des Moustiquaires imprégnées d’insecticides à longue durée d’action (MII) de la préfecture des Lacs se sont réunis, le vendredi 17 juillet à Aného, pour examiner le bilan à mi-parcours. Cette rencontre a permis d’évaluer l’évolution de l’opération sur le terrain, mesurer le taux d’approvisionnement des ménages et peaufiner les stratégies nécessaires pour surmonter les défis identifiés. Il s’agit d’atteindre une couverture intégrale (100 %) des ménages préalablement dénombrés dans la préfecture. Au cours des travaux, les participants ont analysé les goulots d’étranglement qui freinent encore le bon déroulement de l’activité. Ils ont également convenu d’intensifier les actions de sensibilisation afin d’inciter les retardataires à retirer leurs moustiquaires avant la clôture de la campagne. La directrice préfectorale de la Santé des Lacs, Dr Akakpo Kafui Eméfa a relevé que l’opération, démarrée le mardi 14 juillet dernier, affiche des résultats encourageants. « Sur les 195.721 moustiquaires réceptionnées pour cette campagne, 133.647 ont été effectivement distribuées aux ménages, soit un taux de réalisation de 68,3 % », a-t-elle précisé. Dr Akakpo a, par ailleurs, lancé un appel pressant aux détenteurs de coupons qui hésitent encore à se déplacer, les invitant à se rendre dans les centres de santé de leur zone pour s’informer sur les sites de retrait. Le président du CLO des Lacs, Bénissan-Tétévi Datè a invité les différents acteurs à poursuivre les actions de proximité afin d’assurer une large couverture des ménages. Il a rappelé que l’utilisation régulière et correcte des moustiquaires demeure l’un des moyens les plus efficaces de prévention contre le paludisme. ATOP/DK/SED/KYA

items.title: 2027 Elections: Labour Party receives INEC access code
items.description: … Begins Upload of Candidates’ Details By John Alechenu ABUJA — The Labour Party (LP) has confirmed that it has received the required access code from the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to upload the details of its candidates for the 2027 governorship and State House of Assembly elections. The party’s National Publicity Secretary, Ken Asogwa, disclosed this in Abuja on Monday, saying the upload process commenced on Saturday and has been progressing smoothly. Asogwa said the party had already uploaded a significant number of candidates’ details as of Monday morning, while efforts were ongoing to complete the exercise within the timeline provided by INEC. “The Labour Party can confirm that INEC has provided the party with the access code required to upload its governorship and House of Assembly candidates to the INEC nomination portal. “The upload exercise commenced on Saturday and has been progressing seamlessly. As of this morning, we have uploaded a substantial number,” he said. The party spokesman, however, did not disclose the states or names of candidates whose details had been uploaded. He explained that the earlier challenges experienced by political parties during the upload of presidential and National Assembly candidates’ information were largely due to technical issues involving the harmonisation of names on party lists with records on the INEC portal. “A lot of political parties experienced difficulties during the upload of the particulars of presidential and National Assembly candidates. It was not peculiar to Labour Party, as many parties faced the challenge due to the initial difficulty in harmonising names on parties’ lists with those on the INEC portal,” Asogwa said. INEC had earlier released timelines for political parties to submit candidates’ particulars ahead of the 2027 general elections. The Labour Party assured that it was working to ensure compliance with the electoral commission’s requirements and meet the stipulated deadlines across all states. The post 2027 Elections: Labour Party receives INEC access code appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Kayouh : Le Maroc place la sécurité routière au cœur de ses efforts de développement durable
items.description: Le Maroc a placé la sécurité routière au cœur de ses efforts de développement durable, a souligné lundi au siège des Nations unies le ministre du Transport et de la Logistique, Abdessamad Kayouh. “Le Maroc a fait de la sécurité routière un élément important de ses efforts de développement durable” , a déclaré le ministe lors de la Conférence des donateurs du Fonds des Nations unies pour la sécurité routière (FNUSR), tenue en présence de l’ambassadeur, représentant permanent du Maroc auprès des Nations unies, Omar Hilale. Soulignant la fierté du Maroc d’être un partenaire du FNUSR, Kayouh a ajouté qu’en tant que président du Conseil consultatif du Fonds, le Royaume continue “d’appuyer sa mission consistant à sauver des vies grâce à la coopération internationale” . Pour le ministre, la Conférence des donateurs ne porte pas uniquement sur les contributions financières, mais constitue également un espace privilégié pour “travailler ensemble afin de sauver davantage de vies” . Relevant que les motocyclistes figurent parmi les usagers de la route les plus vulnérables, Kayouh a souligné que, pour cette raison, “le Maroc a érigé la sécurité des motocyclistes en priorité nationale” . À cet égard, le ministre a rappelé que, lors de la 4e Conférence ministérielle mondiale sur la sécurité routière, tenue en février 2025 à Marrakech, le Maroc a lancé le Prix international Mohammed VI pour la sécurité routière. Le Royaume a également mis en place, dans la cadre de la même conférence, un Village de la sécurité routière visant à promouvoir le port du casque et à sensibiliser les motocyclistes. Le Maroc a, en outre, soutenu la distribution de casques, a-t-il ajouté. Coorganisée par la France, la Jamaïque, l’Envoyé spécial du Secrétaire général de l’ONU pour la sécurité routière et le Bureau des commissions régionales des Nations unies à New York, la conférence vise à mobiliser 10,6 millions de dollars afin de pérenniser et d’étendre les interventions du Fonds des Nations unies pour la sécurité routière (UNRSF) durant la seconde moitié de la Décennie d’action pour la sécurité routière. La Conférence des donateurs s’est tenue notamment en présence de l’Envoyé spécial du Secrétaire général des Nations unies pour la sécurité routière, Jean Todt, et du directeur général de l’Organisation mondiale de la santé (OMS), Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. The post Kayouh : Le Maroc place la sécurité routière au cœur de ses efforts de développement durable appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Las llamas no dan tregua en La Mierla: 32 pueblos evacuados y 26.000 hectáreas afectadas
items.description: El negro sigue tiñendo la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, donde el fuego iniciado el pasado jueves en La Mierla alcanza ya las 26.000 hectáreas, el más grande en la historia de Castilla-La Mancha, superando el que se produjo en 2005 en La Riba de Saelices, también en Guadalajara, con 13.000 hectáreas, pero mucho más trágico por la muerte de 11 miembros de un retén de Cogolludo. La ausencia de daños personales es la diferencia sustancial entre ambos fuegos porque el de La Mierla alcanza ya un perímetro de 120 kilómetros, 10 de ellos sólo la pasada noche. Las cifras se quedan cortas en cada actualización. Los municipios desalojados son 32, según los datos ofrecidos a mediodía desde el Puesto de Mando Avanzado de Tamajón, por el director del dispositivo de Infocam, Juan José Fernández, que ha confesado que “el incendio está muy lejos de ser controlado”. Las llamas, reconocía Fernández, están cruzando cortafuegos de hasta 40 metros de longitud y los focos que se están reactivando en algunas zonas impiden tener “un control de frente continuo”. Seguir leyendo Castilla y León refuerza sus medios en Soria ante el temor a que el incendio salte a esa provincia El Operativo INFOCAL de la Junta de Castilla y León ha reforzado el despliegue de medios humanos y materiales ante el avance del incendio forestal declarado en La Mierla (Guadalajara) en previsión de que el fuego pueda alcanzar la provincia de Soria durante la tarde de este lunes, informa Juan Navarro. El análisis realizado por los técnicos del INFOCAL apunta a que las llamas "podrían entrar hoy en la provincia de Soria" a través de un "amplio frente" entre los términos municipales de Barcones y Retortillo, aunque su evolución "dependerá de las condiciones meteorológicas y de la eficacia de las labores de extinción desarrolladas durante la tarde. La Junta de Castilla y León está en contacto con los alcaldes de varios municipios de la zona para "coordinar actuaciones protección" en el entorno de estos núcleos población y se trabaja con las organizaciones agrarias para organizar y coordinar la colaboración voluntaria de agricultores mediante tractores con grada y abrir fajas de defensa en puntos previamente establecidos por el operativo.

items.title: US attacks deepen hardship, not dissent, in Iran's south
items.description: Opposition to Iran’s government has long simmered in the country’s impoverished south. But after bearing the brunt of US strikes this month, many residents are directing their anger at President Donald Trump ​and an air campaign they say has upended their lives. Southern Iran, which borders the Strait of Hormuz, has been a major target of US attacks on ports, energy ‌facilities and transport infrastructure. Washington claims the strikes are aimed at curbing Iran’s ability to threaten shipping through the strategic waterway. Tehran says they have gone far beyond legitimate military targets. Most of the more than two dozen southern residents Reuters interviewed by phone or messaging apps agreed that the strikes had gone beyond targets with a clear military purpose. “Trump has ruined our lives,” said Amin, who runs a small boarding house ​for tourists in the south, blaming the US strikes for worsening economic hardship. None of ​those Reuters spoke to said the conflict had generated support for renewed anti-government protests. No new wave of unrest in the south “I feel like I’m ⁠suffocating,” added Amin. Such frustration with the strikes’ impact helps explain why, even in provinces known for anti-government protest, the war has not triggered further unrest — something Trump had hoped for at the start ​of the conflict. Like the other Iranians Reuters spoke to, Amin asked that his family name not be published for fear of reprisals. Reza, 62, a veteran of the 1988-89 Iran-Iraq war, said he was still willing to fight against the United States. “If my country needs me I’ll take up arms again, not for this regime but for the soil beneath my feet. It’s the least I owe it,” said the retired teacher from his home in Abadan in the southwestern Khuzestan province. The southerners Reuters spoke to included people who ​had taken part in previous bouts of protest, but none said they supported renewed unrest for now. Huge region is home to several ethnic minorities Southern Iran’s provinces of Hormozgan, Bushehr, Khuzestan and Sistan-Baluchistan form a large, diverse region stretching along a humid, rugged coastline from Iraq to Pakistan. Khuzestan is home to a large Arab minority, Sistan-Baluchistan to Iran’s Balochi ethnic minority and Hormozgan to a Sunni Muslim religious minority. Despite having most ⁠of Iran’s oil and gas, southerners have also complained of low investment, little opportunity and poor state services. “The US attacks have deepened our problems. No electricity for hours means no water for hours in ​this heat,” said Hadi, a grocery shop owner in Khuzestan province. “I love Iran. This war should stop. Enough is enough,” he added. The authorities deny any regional, ethnic or religious discrimination. Majid, a 41-year-old engineer in Konarak, said the war had brought new fear to a town already struggling with years of ⁠neglect: nights filled ​with the sound of explosions, followed by days of heat, hours of darkness and fears of empty water pipes. Power ​cuts have become especially punishing in provinces where summer temperatures regularly climb above 40 degrees Celsius, leaving families without air conditioning and disrupting water supplies that depend on electric pumps. In Bushehr, home to Iran’s one working nuclear power plant, high ​school student Samad said he could not focus on his exams because of the nightly attacks.

items.title: Exchange rate has remained broadly stable despite global pressures – BoG Governor Asiama
items.description: The Governor of the Bank of Ghana (BoG), Dr Johnson Pandit Asiama, has said the Ghana cedi has remained broadly stable against the major international trading currencies despite persistent global economic uncertainties and external market pressures. Opening the 131st Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting on Monday, July 20, Dr Asiama said recent economic indicators pointed to continued resilience in the foreign exchange market, adding that the committee would carefully evaluate current developments before taking its next policy decision. “The exchange rate has remained broadly stable, supported by improving macroeconomic fundamentals and recent policy measures,” Dr Asiama stated. He added that the committee’s responsibility was to ensure that its policy stance remained appropriate in the face of evolving economic conditions. “Our task this week is not simply to assess the latest data. It is to determine whether the framework we strengthened in May remains fit for the conditions now before us, and whether the choices we made then continue to serve the medium-term objectives on which our credibility depends,” he said.

items.title: Delta gets new private varsity
items.description: By Chancel Bomadi Sunday BOMADI — A new private institution, Zoro International Open University (ZIOU), has been established in Bomadi, headquarters of Bomadi Local Government Area of Delta State, with the aim of expanding access to tertiary education for residents of riverine communities. The university authorities said the institution was created to address the growing educational needs of young people in the riverine areas, where access to higher education has remained a challenge due to geographical limitations. The management of ZIOU disclosed that admission into the university would commence soon, adding that all necessary arrangements and operational requirements for the take-off of academic activities had been completed. The Vice Chancellor of the university, Prof. Osadi Benemone, said the establishment of the institution would provide opportunities for students in riverine towns and villages to pursue higher education without the difficulties often associated with travelling long distances to urban centres. He assured that the university had met the required conditions for commencement and was committed to providing quality education through diverse academic programmes. The institution currently offers programmes across four faculties, including the Faculty of Sciences, with courses in Agricultural Science and Computer Science; Faculty of Arts and Humanities, offering English Language, Philosophy, Psychology, Mass Communication, International Relations and History. Others are the Faculty of Education, with programmes in Educational Management, Public Health Education, Social Studies and Biology Education, as well as the Faculty of Management and Social Sciences, offering Accountancy, Business Administration, Public Administration, Economics, Marketing, Political Science and Criminology. The university management said the institution would continue to expand its programmes and contribute to improving access to tertiary education in Delta State’s riverine communities and beyond. The post Delta gets new private varsity appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: “Pretty Hurts”: The unregulated war on beauty in Ghana – a sister thinking out loud
items.description: Introduction In Accra, Ghana, a stroll through bustling neighbourhoods like East Legon, Osu Oxford Street, Madina, Ablekuma, Spintex or a journey up north to Tamale, Kumasi, or move to the Western city of Takoradi reveals a striking trend: gleaming storefronts and social media adverts promising “perfect curves,” “pink skin,” “shiny teeth,” and “Hollywood smiles’ Flat tummies” . Beauty clinics and spas are multiplying at an unprecedented rate, offering everything from buttock and breast enhancements to flat tummies and fashion braces. Yet beneath these glossy advertisements and seemingly glamourous appearances lies a public health crisis that experts warn is fuelling a surging rate of cancer, kidney disease, hypertension, and other non-communicable diseases (NCDs) across the country (Amoako et al., 2021). Who Is Watching? Regulatory oversight remains alarmingly weak. The Food and Drugs Authority (FDA) Ghana is mandated to regulate cosmetic products, while the Health Facilities Regulatory Agency (HeFRA) licenses health facilities. However, enforcement is inconsistent, and many so-called “beauty clinics” operate under the guise of salons, exploiting legal loopholes (FDA Ghana, 2020). This regulatory gap allows untrained individuals often self-proclaimed beauticians to administer intravenous infusions, inject skin-lightening agents, and perform invasive procedures without medical training or accountability. These practitioners frequently source skin-bleaching creams and injectables from unverified international suppliers, including online marketplaces. Many of these products contain dangerous levels of mercury, hydroquinone, and corticosteroids substances banned or strictly regulated in other countries due to their toxic effects (Nkemngong et al., 2022). Mercury, for instance, is a known nephrotoxin linked to chronic kidney disease, while prolonged hydroquinone use increases the risk of ochronosis and skin malignancies (WHO, 2020). The “Figure 8” Body: A Dangerous Ideal The pursuit of the so-called “figure 8” body characterized by a narrow waist and exaggerated hips has become a global obsession. Women are resorting to extreme measures: starvation, unregulated slimming teas, herbal pills, and topical creams promising rapid fat reduction or localised weight gain for specific parts of the body. Many apply unknown oils and chemical mixtures to enhance buttock size, often without understanding the ingredients or potential health consequences. Home-based vendors, operating with little to no regulation, produce all kinds of oral and topical beauty products like soaps, hips, butts and breast enhancement creams/oils from the comfort of their kitchens and backyards and then sell openly via social media platforms. These high demand products are sold in unlabelled containers with no ingredient list, production and expiry dates, FDA certification details; talk less of dosages or instructions for use. Others are importing weight loss or gain products and openly bragging of having the most affordable and potent products while comparing themselves to each other. The rise of “okada” (motorbike) delivery services has made access easier than ever. Influencers with idealized physiques promote these products, creating a cycle of demand driven by appearance rather than safety or science. Interestingly, many of these influencers never use the products and services they advertise. This obsession ignores biological realities: body shape is influenced by genetics, hormonal balance, metabolism, and nutrition not by miracle creams or dangerous concoctions (Addo et al., 2020). Yet, the pressure to conform to an increasingly narrow beauty standard continues to grow. Fashion Braces: When Aesthetics Override Health One of the most concerning trends is the rise of “fashion braces” cosmetic dental appliances marketed solely for aesthetic enhancement, not orthodontic correction. These cheap, unregulated devices are often imported from dubious online sources and installed in beauty salons and spas by unlicensed personnel. I recently witnessed a disturbing scene: a woman reclining on a salon bench typically used for eyelash extensions receiving braces installation from a beautician who, I later learned, was national service personnel with no dental training and operates a beauty salon as a side hustle. Two other young ladies waited their turn. This normalization of medical procedures in non-clinical settings is a public health red flag. Certified orthodontists warn that improperly fitted braces can cause irreversible damage, including tooth root resorption, periodontal disease, enamel erosion, and even tooth loss (Opoku et al., 2023). Yet, the desire for a “perfect smile” for TikTok, Facebook, and Instagram reels overrides caution. The aestheticization of health interventions turns critical medical care into a fashion accessory with potentially lifelong consequences. Skincare as Social Currency Skincare routines have evolved into a social status symbol. Women are bombarded with messages that not having a multi-step regimen equates to being “laid back” or irresponsible. Influencers promote complex routines involving toners, serums, exfoliants, and bleaching agents often without disclosing ingredients or risks. More troubling is the practice of mixing multiple products sometimes from different brands and chemical compositions into custom “toning” or “brightening” blends. I once observed a vendor in a cosmetic shop advertising a “bridal glow package” promising “caramel, mulatto, and half-cast” skin tones days before weddings. These mixtures, created without regard for chemical interactions, are sold with glowing testimonials audio and video “proof” of transformation yet their long-term effects remain unknown. Even more alarming is the bleaching of infants and toddlers. Some parents apply skin-lightening creams on newborns, believing lighter skin offers social and economic advantages (Boakye, 2022). This practice reflects deep-seated colorism rooted in colonial legacies and perpetuated by media and advertising. Meanwhile, natural, safe, and effective alternatives like raw shea butter, cocoa butter, cold-pressed coconut oil, and olive oil ingredients foundational to many commercial products are being abandoned in favor of branded, chemically-laden alternatives. Ghanaian-owned brands like *Skin Gourmet*, which prioritize natural formulations, offer a healthier, culturally rooted alternative, a brand I have personally used for years. Why Are Women Risking Their Lives? Is this merely low self-esteem? The answer is more complex. Body shaming is rampant in Ghanaian society. From family gatherings where relatives comment on a woman’s flat chest or dark skin, to university campuses where skin tone dictates social inclusion, the psychological pressure is relentless. Social media amplifies this, with anonymous users dissecting women’s bodies in comment sections. Influencers many of whom use filters, editing tools, or even undergo undisclosed procedures promote unattainable beauty standards. The advertising industry profits from this insecurity, turning women’s bodies into projects to be “fixed.” As one young woman confided, “If I don’t enhance my body, no man will want me.” This belief, though deeply personal, is structurally reinforced by cultural norms, media narratives, and economic incentives. The Cost of “Pretty” The human cost of this beauty war is staggering: Skin lightening (“toning”) is linked to chronic kidney disease, mercury poisoning, and skin cancers (Nkemngong et al., 2022). Unregulated butt injections, often using industrial silicone or unsterile fillers, have caused pulmonary embolisms, infections, and deaths (Amoako et al., 2021). Herbal “detox” drips administered by untrained hands can lead to septic shock, air embolisms, and IV site infections. Fashion braces and DIY teeth whitening are destroying healthy dentition. Despite these risks, clinics continue to multiply, and customers keep coming driven by a culture that equates beauty with worth. A Way Forward 1. Strengthen Regulation The FDA and HeFRA must be empowered with clearer mandates, increased staffing, and greater enforcement capacity. A publicly accessible registry of licensed beauty and cosmetic practitioners should be established to enable consumer verification. Customs agencies must intensify screening of imported cosmetic products, particularly those shipped via informal channels. 2. Public Education Campaigns The Ministry of Health, Ghana Health Service, and National Commission for Civic Education should launch nationwide campaigns on the dangers of unregulated cosmetic procedures, skin bleaching, and DIY treatments. These should include school curricula, community outreach, and media partnerships. 3. Hold Influencers Accountable Social media influencers who promote cosmetic products must disclose whether they use them and whether they are certified by health authorities. Platforms like Instagram and TikTok could be pressured to remove content promoting dangerous practices. 4. Cultural Shift Change must also be cultural. Family and friends must stop body-shaming. Men must learn to celebrate women as they are. Women must reclaim agency over their bodies not as projects to be perfected, but as sites of strength, identity, and pride. My Body, My Pride Pride is not the rejection of self-improvement. It is the rejection of shame as a marketing tool. It is the courage to ask, *“What’s in this cream?” or “Are you licensed to inject me?”. It is understanding that real beauty does not require dialysis, reconstructive surgery, or a visit to the oncology ward. Pretty hurts. But it doesn’t have to kill you. Your health is not a trend. References Addo, P. C., Osei, K., & Boateng, A. (2020). Body image perception and its determinants among young women in urban Ghana. *Ghana Medical Journal, 54*(3), 167–174. https://doi.org/10.4314/gmj.v54i3.5 Amoako, A. A., Asamoah, B. O., & Frimpong, J. B. (2021). The rising trend of cosmetic surgery and its implications for public health in Ghana. *BMC Public Health, 21*(1), 1–9. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12889-021-10945-4 Boakye, Y. (2022). *Colourism and skin bleaching in Ghana: Historical roots and contemporary realities*. African Studies Quarterly, 21(2), 45–60. Food and Drugs Authority (FDA) Ghana. (2020). *Guidelines for the regulation of cosmetic products in Ghana*. FDA Ghana. Nkemngong, M. A., Umoh, A. S., & Ajoke, C. A. (2022). Mercury and hydroquinone levels in skin-lightening creams marketed in Ghana and Nigeria: Public health implications. *Journal of Environmental and Public Health, 2022*, 1–8. https://doi.org/10.1155/2022/9786543 Opoku, M. P., Nyako, E. A., & Ofori-Asenso, R. (2023). Orthodontic tourism and the rise of “fashion braces” in urban Ghana: A call for regulation. *BMC Oral Health, 23*(1), 1–7. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12903-023-03058-5 World Health Organization (WHO). (2020). *Mercury in skin lightening products: A global review of legislation and enforcement*. WHO Press. https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240014748 Note: All names and specific locations used in personal observations are based on the author’s lived experience and are not intended to identify individuals or businesses for legal action. Written by Dorcas Zenabu Seidu Program Manager – Public Health Nursing – Office of Director Nursing and Midwifery – GHS Executive Assistant – DNMS

items.title: BoG opens 131st MPC meeting amid global economic uncertainties, energy price pressures
items.description: The Governor of the Bank of Ghana (BoG), Dr Johnson Pandit Asiama, has opened the 131st Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting with a call for careful, evidence-based policy decisions as Ghana navigates global economic uncertainty and rising energy prices. Addressing the opening session on Monday, July 20, Dr Asiama said the Committee would assess recent inflation trends, the impact of the Bank’s May monetary policy reforms, domestic liquidity conditions and external risks facing the economy. He noted that although Ghana’s economy has continued to demonstrate resilience, policymakers must remain vigilant in responding to evolving global and domestic developments. “Our task this week is not simply to assess the latest data. It is to determine whether the framework we strengthened in May remains fit for the conditions now before us, and whether the choices we made then continue to serve the medium-term objectives on which our credibility depends,” the Governor stated. Dr Asiama also launched the inaugural Monetary Policy Committee Educational Observership Programme (MPC-EOP), welcoming students from the University of Ghana to observe aspects of the MPC process. He said the initiative reflects the Bank’s commitment to transparency, public engagement and developing the next generation of economists and policymakers.

items.title: Police arrest 5 suspected robbers in Accra, retrieve firearms and ammunition
items.description: The Accra Regional Police Command has arrested five people suspected of being members of a criminal group involved in armed robbery and landguard activities following an intelligence-led operation at Sapeiman Faase in the Greater Accra Region. The operation, carried out jointly by the Accra Regional Police Command and the National Police Intelligence Directorate (NPID), led to the recovery of firearms, ammunition and other offensive items believed to have been used to terrorise residents in the area. According to a statement issued by the Head of Public Affairs of the Accra Regional Police Command, Chief Superintendent Juliana Obeng, the suspects were arrested on July 17, 2026. The suspects have been identified as Torgbui Agah Semaglo, 50; Shadrach Agah Semaglo, 22; William Kweku Sedanu, 21; Cyril Kassenu, 26; and Kingsford Agah Semaglo, 18. Police said a search of the suspects’ premises resulted in the recovery of several items, including a pump-action shotgun, a Stoeger ST-R9 pistol, a dummy Austria pistol, a jackknife, a face mask, 12 BB cartridges, three rounds of 9mm ammunition, one round of AK-47 ammunition, a talisman and two kitchen knives. The statement said all the retrieved items have been retained as exhibits for investigations. Preliminary investigations, according to the Police, indicate that the suspects are believed to be members of a criminal syndicate engaged in armed robbery and landguard activities. “Preliminary investigations indicate that the suspects are believed to be members of a criminal group involved in armed robbery and landguard activities and are alleged to have used the recovered weapons to terrorise residents within the Sapeiman area and its environs,” the statement said. The Police said the suspects remain in custody and are assisting with investigations while efforts continue to determine whether they are linked to other robbery cases and criminal activities. “The suspects are currently in Police custody assisting with investigations, while efforts are underway to establish their involvement in other criminal activities and to apprehend any additional accomplices,” the statement added. The Accra Regional Police Command commended members of the public for providing credible information that supports police operations and appealed for continued cooperation in the fight against crime. “The Accra Regional Police Command commends members of the public who continue to support the Police with credible information and urges residents to remain vigilant and promptly report suspicious persons or activities to the nearest Police Station or through the Police emergency numbers,” the statement said.

items.title: Dubréka : deux enfants meurent noyés dans une fosse septique abandonnée
items.description: Le drame s'est produit dans le district de Bensenkén, secteur Horoya, relevant de la préfecture de Dubréka, où deux enfants d'une même famille ont perdu la vie après être tombés dans une fosse septique non couverte. Selon les informations recueillies auprès de témoins, les deux enfants avaient quitté le domicile familial le vendredi 17 juillet 2026, aux environs de 16 heures. Leur absence prolongée avait aussitôt suscité l'inquiétude de leurs parentss, qui avaient entrepris des recherches avant de signaler leur disparition aux autorités compétentes. Après deux jours de recherches, les corps sans vie des deux enfants ont finalement été découverts dans une fosse septique abandonnée. Il s'agit de Hadja Aissatou Kindy Barry et d'Abdoulaye Barry, âgé de 10 ans. Selon leur mère, Zeibab Barry, les deux victimes seraient décédées par noyade après avoir chuté dans cette fosse laissée à ciel ouvert. « Au moment où je priais, mes enfants sont sortis de la maison. Après plusieurs heures sans les voir revenir, je suis partie à leur recherche. J'ai ensuite fait une déclaration de disparition. Ce n'est que deux jours plus tard que leurs corps ont été retrouvés sans vie dans une fosse septique », a confié la mère des victimes, visiblement bouleversée. Informées du drame, les autorités locales se sont rendues auprès de la famille endeuillée afin de lui présenter leurs condoléances et de lui témoigner leur soutien. Les corps des deux enfants ont ensuite été remis à leurs proches pour leur inhumation.

items.title: 33 years after establishment, NINLAN set to receive take-off grant — Executive Director
items.description: By Ugochukwu Alaribe UMUAHIA — The Executive Director of the National Institute for Nigerian Languages (NINLAN), Aba, Abia State, Prof. Ogbonna Onuoha, has disclosed that the institution is set to receive a long-awaited take-off grant from the Federal Government to address its infrastructural challenges. NINLAN, which is affiliated with Nnamdi Azikiwe University, Awka, was established in 1993 as Nigeria’s apex institution for the research, teaching and award of degrees and diplomas in Nigerian languages. Speaking during the institute’s 8th matriculation ceremony, Prof. Onuoha said positive developments had begun to emerge at NINLAN, following the return of support from the Tertiary Education Trust Fund (TETFund) and the Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB), with the approval of the Federal Government. He explained that the take-off grant, which the institution has not received since its establishment 33 years ago, would significantly boost ongoing efforts to improve infrastructure and enable NINLAN to effectively fulfil its mandate as a centre for language research and training. “We have not gotten a take-off grant since NINLAN was established 33 years ago. I am not blaming anyone, but the indications I have received during my 18 months in office suggest that the Federal Government will soon release the grant,” he said. According to him, the funding would support critical projects, including perimeter fencing, internal roads and other infrastructural developments across the institute’s campuses. He noted that NINLAN’s main campus covers about 209.5 hectares of land, adding that opening up the area through infrastructure development would create opportunities for further expansion. Prof. Onuoha also revealed that the institute had secured accreditation from the National Universities Commission (NUC) and the National Council on Education for its degree, diploma and certificate programmes. He assured that NINLAN possesses the human and academic resources required to maintain high standards, adding that the institution currently has 60 fully equipped language laboratories to support teaching and research. The Executive Director expressed appreciation to the Federal Government, particularly the Ministry of Education, for including NINLAN in TETFund interventions, noting that ongoing projects, including faculty buildings, would further strengthen academic activities at the institute. He said some projects were expected to be completed within six months, while additional developments would commence soon. Onuoha also commended the Minister of Education for policies aimed at transforming Nigeria’s education sector, saying the ministry’s support had contributed significantly to renewed activities at NINLAN. He equally appreciated the Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Chief Benjamin Kalu, and Abia State Governor, Dr. Alex Otti, for their support towards the growth and development of the institute. The post 33 years after establishment, NINLAN set to receive take-off grant — Executive Director appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: ‘Legendary’ former Liverpool and England star Keegan dies aged 75
items.description: Former Liverpool and England star Kevin Keegan has died aged 75 after battling cancer, his family announced on Monday. Keegan, regarded as one of the greatest players of his generation, was diagnosed with stage four cancer and underwent treatment in January. “It is with immense sadness that we announce that Kevin Keegan has passed away at the age of 75,” a statement from Keegan’s family said. “The former England player and manager had been battling cancer and was surrounded by his wife and daughters in his final moments.” Keegan, a dynamic striker nicknamed “mighty mouse”, won the English top-flight title three times with Liverpool after starting his legendary career with lowly Scunthorpe. He also won the European Cup in 1977 and the FA Cup in 1974 during an iconic six-year spell at Anfield before joining German club Hamburg in 1977. Keegan, who scored 21 goals in 63 games for England, won the Bundesliga title with Hamburg in 1979 and helped them reach the European Cup final the following year. He was voted the European Player of the Year in 1978 and 1979. Keegan returned to England to play for Southampton and Newcastle, retiring in 1984 after starring in the Magpies’ promotion to the top tier. “We are devastated to learn of the passing of Kevin Keegan, one of the most iconic, influential and deeply-loved figures in our club’s history,” Newcastle said in a statement. “Kevin was more than a legendary footballer and manager. He was the beating heart of Newcastle United for generations of supporters. “As a player, he brought world-class talent, ambition and belief to St James’ Park, helping to transform perceptions of what Newcastle United could be. “As a manager, he ignited our city. Kevin gave supporters hope and pride, and led a team whose style, spirit and passion captured the imagination of football fans around the world. He was so much more than an entertainer.” – Keegan’s ‘entertainers’ – Southampton added: “We are devastated to learn of the passing of Kevin Keegan. Kevin signed for the club in 1980 as the holder of the Ballon d’Or and brought great joy to fans at The Dell and across the football world.” Turning to management in 1992, he led Newcastle to promotion to the Premier League in 1993. Keegan established them as a force in the top-flight, dubbed “the entertainers” as they finished runners-up to Manchester United after blowing a big lead in 1995-96 title race. His clashes with United manager Alex Ferguson — including his famous “I’d love it if we beat them” rant — took a toll on Keegan, who eventually resigned in 1997. He went on to manage Fulham before taking charge of England in a turbulent spell from 1999 to 2000, quitting the role of national team boss just hours after a 1-0 defeat against Germany at Wembley. England had failed to make it out of the group stage at Euro 2000 during Keegan’s reign. Keegan also spent four years as Manchester City manager before finishing his managerial career with a brief return to Newcastle in 2008. “Kevin, a double Ballon d’Or winner, was a much loved husband, father and grandfather. The family would like to thank Kevin’s incredible medical team for all their support,” his family’s statement added. “This is a hugely difficult time and they are requesting space and privacy.” The post ‘Legendary’ former Liverpool and England star Keegan dies aged 75 appeared first on Vanguard News .
